Navigate to other books or search results:
1) User clicks 'More in this genre' in the header or related books section.
2) Component reads currentBook.genre and navigates to books.html?genre={currentBook.genre}.
3) If a 'Back to results' link is present and the original source provided information via an additional URL parameter such as from=books or from=library, the component uses this in navigation logic (e.g., history.back() or explicit URL reconstruction) without hardcoding URLs beyond the allowed navigation patterns.
interactions: Initial page load: On page initialization, the controller reads the id URL parameter. If present, it calls getBookDetail(bookId=id). While awaiting the response, the controller can render a loading state. On success, it passes book, reviewSummary, and relatedBooks to child components. If id is missing or getBookDetail fails, the page shows an error state with no hardcoded text content and may suppress dependent actions.

Change book via related books: When a user clicks on a related book in the related books component, the browser navigates to book_detail.html?id={clickedBook.id}. This triggers a full reload of the page controller, which again calls getBookDetail with the new id and resets internal review pagination and sorting state unless overridden by URL parameters.

Add to favorites interaction: When the favorites button is clicked, the library actions component calls addBookToFavorites(currentBook.id). Based on the returned success flag and message, it updates UI feedback (success or error). The favoritesList and listItem returned can be used to enable a direct 'View favorites list' navigation by linking to library.html?listId={favoritesList.id}.

Open 'Save to list' interaction: Clicking 'Save to list' opens a modal. Inside the modal, selecting a list type triggers getLibraryLists(list_type) and populates a list selector. Selecting a specific LibraryList and confirming triggers addBookToLibraryList(listId, bookId). UI feedback is based on the response’s success and message.

Create list within modal: In the same modal, the user can submit a small form with name, list_type, and description. On submit, the component calls createLibraryList(name, list_type, description). On success, it immediately calls addBookToLibraryList(newList.id, currentBook.id), and updates the modal state and confirmation messaging. The 'View list' button uses the returned list id for navigation.

Add to bundle interaction: When 'Add to bundle' is clicked, the purchase actions component calls addBookToBundle(currentBook.id). On success, it updates any bundle status indicators and displays the returned bundleSummary.item_count, bundleSummary.total_price, and bundleSummary.currency. If the call fails, it shows the message field in an error state.

Add to cart interaction: When 'Add to cart' is clicked, the purchase actions component reads the selected quantity from internal state (defaulting to 1 if no selector exists) and calls addBookToCart(currentBook.id, quantity). On success, the component updates cart indicators (e.g., item count) using cartSummary.item_count and provides a 'Go to cart' button that navigates to cart.html?from=book_detail.

Reviews pagination interaction: Pagination controls (e.g., next/previous, specific page numbers) trigger an update of a logical currentPage state and also update the reviews_page URL parameter. The component then calls getBookReviews(currentBook.id, page=currentPage, page_size=currentPageSize, sort_by=currentSort). When the response arrives, it replaces the review list with the new reviews array.

Reviews sorting interaction: The sort control (e.g., dropdown) changes a logical currentSort state and updates the reviews_sort URL parameter. It then calls getBookReviews with the updated sort_by value while resetting page to 1 (and updating reviews_page accordingly).

Submit review interaction: The review form’s onSubmit handler validates the input (e.g., rating presence, body non-empty) in the UI layer and then calls submitBookReview(bookId, rating, title, body, reviewer_name). On success, it clears the form fields, shows a transient success confirmation, and triggers either a re-fetch of reviews via getBookReviews or inserts the returned review into the current reviews array. Optionally, it may call getBookDetail again to refresh reviewSummary.

Navigation to Books & Search: Clicking 'More in this genre' constructs a URL of the form books.html?genre={currentBook.genre} without hardcoding the genre value. Clicking 'Back to results' may use stored URL parameters like fromGenre or referrerGenre if the application appends them; otherwise, it can fall back to books.html?genre={currentBook.genre}.

Navigation to Library: After actions that return a LibraryList (addBookToFavorites or createLibraryList / addBookToLibraryList), a 'View list' action navigates to library.html?listId={returnedList.id}. This uses only data from API responses, not hardcoded IDs.
Navigation to Cart: After addBookToCart succeeds, clicking 'View cart' or 'Go to cart' navigates to cart.html?from=book_detail using the prescribed outgoing navigation pattern.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary page identity is determined by the id URL parameter (book_detail.html?id={bookId}). When id changes (via navigation or direct URL), the page controller must reload book data by calling getBookDetail(bookId=id). Derived or secondary state is also reflected in URL parameters to avoid localStorage: 1) Reviews pagination state is stored as reviews_page. When this parameter is present and valid, the reviews component uses it as the current page; otherwise it defaults to 1. 2) Reviews sort state is stored as reviews_sort (e.g., a backend-supported sort key). When present, it is passed to getBookReviews as sort_by; otherwise a default sort is used. 3) Optionally, a page_size parameter can be read to set the number of reviews per page, passed to getBookReviews as page_size; if not present, the component uses a default configurable value. 4) Navigation context may be preserved using optional URL parameters like from, referrerGenre, or referrerListId when linking into book_detail.html from other pages, but the Book Detail page itself only relies on id for data loading and uses any additional parameters solely to influence navigation targets (e.g., deciding whether to show a tailored 'Back to results' link). 5) All data that changes as a result of user actions (favorites, lists, bundles, cart, reviews) is reflected in the UI using the responses from the assigned interfaces and not persisted in localStorage or other client-side storage. Any necessary cross-page persistence relies on the backend via the provided APIs; the Book Detail page assumes the backend keeps consistent state for subsequent requests (e.g., reloading reviews after a new review submission).

components.functionality: Controls the lifecycle of the Book Detail page: reads URL parameters, triggers data fetches via getBookDetail and getBookReviews, distributes loaded data to child components, and coordinates navigation to other pages.

Displays key information about the book (title, subtitle, format, genre, work_type, price, currency, page_count, publication_date, description, cover image) along with the review summary (average rating and rating count) and provides access points for actions such as 'More in this genre' and an anchor to the reviews section.

Provides purchase-related actions for the current book: selecting quantity (if implemented), adding the book to the cart via addBookToCart, and adding the book to an eBook bundle via addBookToBundle. Displays resulting summaries and offers navigation to the cart.

Lets the user add the current book to favorites or save it to a reading list or wishlist. Uses addBookToFavorites for the favorites flow and opens the list selector modal to use getLibraryLists, createLibraryList, and addBookToLibraryList. Shows per-action feedback and enables navigation to library.html with the relevant listId.

Appears when the user chooses to save the book to a list. Allows selecting an existing list (loaded via getLibraryLists(list_type)) or creating a new list (via createLibraryList), then calls addBookToLibraryList(listId, bookId) to actually save the book. Returns the resulting LibraryList and LibraryListItem back to the parent for feedback and navigation.

Coordinates review listing, pagination, sorting, and review submission. Reads URL parameters for review state (reviews_page, reviews_sort, page_size), calls getBookReviews to load data, and uses submitBookReview to add new reviews. Optionally triggers a refresh of the reviewSummary via getBookDetail after successful review submission.

Provides UI controls (e.g., dropdown or buttons) to change the sort order of reviews. When the user selects a new sort option, updates the reviews_sort URL parameter and notifies the reviews-section, which then calls getBookReviews with the new sort_by value.

Displays the list of reviews for the current book along with pagination controls. Binds to the reviews array from getBookReviews and uses total, page, and page_size to calculate pagination. User interactions with pagination controls update URL parameters and trigger re-fetching via getBookReviews.

Provides fields for star rating, optional title, review body, and optional reviewer name. On submit, calls submitBookReview(bookId, rating, title, body, reviewer_name). Updates UI based on success or error and notifies the reviews-section to refresh the review list and summary as needed.

Shows related books using the relatedBooks array from getBookDetail. Each related book item is clickable to navigate to its own Book Detail page via book_detail.html?id={bookId}. May also provide a 'More in this genre' action that uses the current book’s genre to navigate to books.html?genre={genre}.

Provides context-related navigation links such as 'Back to results', 'More in this genre', 'View cart', and 'View list' (when relevant listId is available). Builds URLs only from dynamic values (currentBook.genre, returned LibraryList.id, static target page filenames) and appends prescribed parameters like from or listId.

Displays transient or persistent messages based on responses from actions like addBookToCart, addBookToBundle, addBookToFavorites, addBookToLibraryList, createLibraryList, and submitBookReview. Ensures user receives confirmation or error feedback without hardcoded message content.

Synchronizes key UI state with URL parameters (id, reviews_page, reviews_sort, page_size, and optional navigation context parameters). Listens for changes in these parameters and notifies relevant components to re-fetch or re-render as needed, ensuring the page can be reloaded or shared with state preserved.

layout.grid_system: Fixed-width, center-aligned layout likely based on a 12-column grid, with a two-column main content area inside a full-width background.
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps a strong top-to-bottom structure: a full-width dark header with site title and main navigation at the very top, followed by (optionally) a thin notification strip, and then a single, large, centered black content panel framed by the textured paper background. Inside this panel, the layout uses a two-column grid: the left column is a tall, portrait-like book information area; the right column is a stacked utility column with purchase actions and library management controls at the top and the reviews experience below. A horizontal book-specific navigation row sits at the top of the panel, and a related-books strip anchors the bottom of the panel, mirroring the anchored feeling of the contact form area in the mockup.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps a strong top-to-bottom structure: a full-width dark header with site title and main navigation at the very top, followed by (optionally) a thin notification strip, and then a single, large, centered black content panel framed by the textured paper background. Inside this panel, the layout uses a two-column grid: the left column is a tall, portrait-like book information area; the right column is a stacked utility column with purchase actions and library management controls at the top and the reviews experience below. A horizontal book-specific navigation row sits at the top of the panel, and a related-books strip anchors the bottom of the panel, mirroring the anchored feeling of the contact form area in the mockup.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header consumes roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport height, with generous breathing room around the site title and primary nav. Below it, the central black panel occupies most of the remaining height, inset from the viewport edges by about 32–48px on all sides to expose the crumpled paper background. Inside this panel, the left column takes about 40–45% of the inner width for the book-main-info, and the right column about 55–60% for purchase actions and reviews. Within the right column, the purchase and library actions cluster uses roughly the upper 25–30% of the above-the-fold height; the reviews-section then grows to fill the remaining vertical space. The related-books strip sits at the bottom, spanning the full internal width of the black panel and using about 20–25% of its vertical space when visible.
layout_strategies.content_density: The header and outer margins are relatively spacious, with larger vertical gaps (24–32px) to emphasize the dramatic, literary mood. Inside the central black panel, content density increases: the two-column area is information-dense with compact vertical spacing (8–16px) between elements and tight padding (16–24px) inside cards. The right column in particular—purchase actions, library actions, and reviews—follows the compact, stacked feel of the contact form in the mockup, while still observing the base spacing scale so it doesn’t feel cluttered. The related-books strip is moderately dense, with covers placed closely but not touching, echoing a bookshelf feel.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye first lands on the bright, centered site/author name and main navigation in the header; the current high-level nav item (e.g., ‘Books’) is highlighted in red, echoing the red ‘CONTACT’ from the mockup. From there, the eye drops into the black panel, hitting the book title and cover on the left and almost immediately sliding horizontally to the high-contrast purchase actions on the right (mirroring the portrait-to-subscription pull). The primary buy button and key status messages anchor attention in the upper right. From that focal point, the red ‘Reviews’ heading draws the eye downward into the reviews-section, where the gaze follows an F-shaped pattern: across the sort controls, down the review list, and finally to the bottom-right ‘Submit Review’ button. After completing this path, the gaze naturally drifts down to the full-width related-books strip, which provides an exit path to other titles.

layout.overall_description: The Book Detail page sits over a textured, crumpled paper background, with all main content contained in a fixed-width, center-aligned black panel framed by generous margins. At the top of the viewport, a full-width dark header carries the author or site name in bold, serif typography, flanked or underlined by a horizontal navigation bar where the current section is highlighted in red. Just below this, a slim notification bar can appear as a red or dark strip delivering feedback messages. The main black panel begins beneath this header, its edges crisp and slightly inset from the viewport to reveal the paper texture. Inside the panel, a subtle horizontal row of book navigation links, styled as small, sharp typographic links with a red indicator for the current view, spans the width near the top. Below, the layout resolves into two columns: the left is a tall, portrait-like book-main-info area with the cover image dominating the upper portion and the title, series, author, and metadata stacked beneath in compact blocks; the right column is a vertical stack of tightly grouped cards. The top of the right column holds the book-purchase-actions card—a dark, bordered module with price, format options, and a strong red primary button—followed immediately by a slightly more subdued book-library-actions panel for favorites, lists, and bundles. A bolder red ‘Reviews’ heading then ushers users into the reviews-section, where sorting controls sit like a toolbar above a vertical stack of review cards, culminating in a compact review submission form with its submit button anchored at the bottom-right. As the user scrolls, the black panel continues downward, and at its base the two-column structure gives way to a full-width related-books strip: a horizontal band of book covers laid out like a dark gallery shelf. Modal interactions, such as managing custom lists, appear centered over everything as dark, bordered overlays, further reinforcing the dramatic, literary aesthetic while keeping the underlying fixed-width structure intact.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page shell spans the full viewport, applying the crumpled paper texture to the entire background and framing a single, fixed-width black content panel in the center. The black panel is horizontally centered and inset from the top and bottom by roughly 32–48px, creating a clear margin of background texture around it. Within this panel, the shell establishes a 12-column grid and defines the main two-column layout: the left 5 out of 12 columns (approximately 40–45% of the panel width) reserved for the book-main-info, and the right 7 out of 12 columns (approximately 55–60%) reserved for purchase actions and reviews. The shell also reserves a thin horizontal strip at the top of the black panel for the book-navigation-links and a full-width band at the bottom for the related-books-strip. All page scrolling occurs within this overall frame, so the black panel feels like a solid, central stage on top of a stationary paper backdrop.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard flow container that scrolls with the page; the background texture may be fixed while the centered black panel and its contents scroll vertically.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The book-main-info block occupies the entire left column of the black panel, from just below the internal book-navigation-links row downwards. It spans approximately 40–45% of the panel width, with 16–24px of internal padding. The book cover image is aligned to the top-left of this block, taking up the full width of the column and roughly the upper half of its initial height, establishing a strong vertical, portrait-like focal element analogous to the author portrait in the mockup. Immediately beneath the cover, the book title appears in large, high-contrast typography, followed by a stacked arrangement of subtitle, series name, author name, genre, and rating, each separated by compact vertical gaps of 8–12px. The bottom of the book-main-info column aligns roughly with the middle of the reviews-section on the right for a balanced vertical composition, and extra book metadata can extend the left column downward as needed, remaining in sync with the overall two-column rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block within the left column that scrolls with the page; height grows with content but maintains its left-column footprint.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The book-purchase-actions panel sits at the top of the right column, horizontally aligned with the top of the book-main-info block. It spans the full width of the right column (about 55–60% of the panel width) and is presented as a distinct card with a slightly lighter black or very dark gray background, a thin border, and 16–24px internal padding. Vertical stacking inside this panel is compact: price and format options at the top, followed by availability messaging and a prominent red primary button (e.g., ‘Add to Cart’) centered or right-aligned. Secondary actions like ‘Buy Now’ or ‘Read Sample’ sit immediately below, with 8–12px spacing. Overall, this block occupies roughly the upper 25–30% of the visible right column above the fold, mirroring the top-right subscription box in the mockup and acting as the major right-side focal point.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scrolls with the page, but can optionally become sticky within the right column so that the primary purchase button remains visible until the user scrolls past the reviews-section; it never overlaps the header or the bottom of the black panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Directly beneath the book-purchase-actions card in the right column, separated by a tight vertical gap of about 8–12px, the book-library-actions panel spans the same width and appears as a slightly more subdued card—similar dark background, but with less aggressive coloring than the primary purchase button. Internally, its controls (e.g., ‘Add to Favorites’, ‘Add to List’, ‘Add to Bundle’) are arranged in a compact vertical stack or a couple of short horizontal rows, each control separated by 8px. The panel’s overall height is modest, roughly one-third to one-half the height of the purchase-actions card, so that together they form a compact ‘action cluster’ at the top-right, visually equivalent to the stacked subscription area in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block stacked under the purchase panel; it moves with page scroll and maintains alignment with the right column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When invoked, the library-list-selector-modal appears centered over the entire viewport, overlaying the header, notification bar, and black content panel with a semi-transparent black scrim that dims the background. The modal itself is a rectangular dark card occupying roughly 40–50% of the viewport width and up to 60% of the viewport height, with 24px internal padding and a clear, sharp border. It is horizontally and vertically centered, with its top edge roughly aligned around the upper third of the viewport to avoid feeling too low. Inside, list options are stacked vertically with compact spacing (8–12px), and the primary action button is aligned to the bottom-right of the modal, echoing the right-anchored buttons elsewhere on the page. The modal stands out as a focused, dramatic element against the dimmed, still-visible book detail content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position overlay; it is anchored to the viewport, not the scrollable content, and prevents background scrolling while open.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The reviews-section is anchored in the right column beneath the action cluster. After a slightly larger vertical gap of 16–24px below the book-library-actions panel, a bold ‘Reviews’ heading in red or high-contrast white introduces the section, visually echoing the ‘Send a Message’ heading from the mockup. This heading is left-aligned within the right column. Immediately under it, the review-sort-controls form a shallow horizontal bar, and below that the review-list and review-form stack vertically. The reviews-section extends downward for as long as needed, forming the bulk of the right column’s content and scrolling independently of the relatively fixed-feeling left column. Its left edge aligns precisely with the right column grid line, and its bottom edge sits above the related-books strip, leaving a margin of about 24–32px to visually separate the body content from the lower recommendation band.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard vertical section that scrolls with the page; its internal content (list and form) determines its height.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Placed at the very top of the reviews-section, just 8–12px below the ‘Reviews’ heading, the review-sort-controls are arranged as a slim horizontal bar spanning the full width of the right column. The controls (e.g., a sort dropdown, rating filter, or tabs) are left-aligned and grouped tightly, with 8px gaps between elements. The bar’s height is modest—roughly the height of a single form field—so that it reads as a lightweight toolbar and does not overshadow the reviews themselves. A subtle bottom border or a 4–8px gap separates this control bar from the first review in the review-list, making the hierarchy clear but compact.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline element within the reviews-section, fixed at the top of that section; scrolls with the reviews content without any sticky behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The review-list sits immediately below the review-sort-controls, spanning the full width of the right column, with a small 8–12px vertical gap separating it from the controls. Each individual review appears as a compact, full-width block with the reviewer name, rating, and date on the top line, followed by the review body text. Reviews are stacked vertically with 12–16px of spacing between them, producing a moderately dense column reminiscent of stacked form fields in the mockup. The list grows downward until it reaches a natural limit (e.g., page length or pagination), after which the review-form appears. If the list is long, it occupies the bulk of the visible right column area, with the form accessible by scrolling further downward.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard vertical list within the reviews-section; expands with content and scrolls normally.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The review-form is positioned at the bottom of the reviews-section, directly beneath the review-list. It is separated from the last review by a slightly larger spacing of 16–24px or a subtle divider line, signaling a shift from reading to input. The form spans the full width of the right column and uses a compact, two-row layout inspired by the contact form in the mockup: a top band with shorter fields (e.g., review title and rating) arranged side by side with 8–12px between them, and a bottom band where the main review textarea occupies nearly the full width. Internal vertical spacing between fields is tight (8–12px), aligning with the dense, functional feel of the mockup’s form. The primary ‘Submit Review’ button is anchored at the bottom-right corner of the form area, with a 16px margin from the edges, visually echoing the bottom-right ‘Send’ button seen in the contact design.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard form block that extends the reviews-section; scrolls with the page and expands slightly if validation messages are shown.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The related-books-strip occupies a dedicated band at the bottom of the black panel, spanning its full internal width beneath both the left and right columns. It is separated from the main content above (ending of the reviews-section and, if present, the bottom of book-main-info) by a generous vertical gap of about 24–32px, making it feel like a distinct section. Within this strip, book covers are laid out in a horizontal row (or slight grid), each cover roughly the same height as the book-main-info cover thumbnail but smaller in width, with 16px gaps between items. The strip’s height is constrained so that about one to one-and-a-half rows of related books are visible, echoing a dark, horizontal gallery at the base of the page. A low-contrast section heading like ‘You may also like’ appears at the top-left of this strip, with its text aligned to the left edge of the content grid.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block at the end of the main content; it scrolls into view after the user passes the primary book and review content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The book-navigation-links form a narrow horizontal row near the top of the black panel, immediately inside its top edge and above the two-column content. This row spans the full width of the panel and sits about 16–24px below the bottom of the global header (and any notification bar), acting as a local navigation band for the book detail (e.g., ‘Overview’, ‘Reviews’, ‘About the Author’). Links are left-aligned, using tight horizontal spacing (16–24px between items), with the active view indicated by a red label and/or a thin underline, echoing the highlighted ‘CONTACT’ in the mockup. The row’s height is modest, roughly matching the height of a single line of text plus 8px padding above and below. The two main columns—the book-main-info on the left and the purchase/reviews column on the right—begin directly beneath this nav row, aligning vertically to form a clean transition from navigation to content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static horizontal nav element within the black panel; it scrolls out of view as the user moves down the page and does not stick to the top.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The notification-bar appears as a slim, full-width strip directly beneath the global header and above the top edge of the black content panel. It runs from the left to the right edge of the viewport, or at least matches the width of the central content area, and has a height of about 40–56px. When active, it uses a strong background color—often a deep red for errors or confirmations—with white text, in keeping with the dramatic color palette. The message text is center-aligned or left-aligned with generous horizontal padding (16–24px), and icons (like a checkmark or alert symbol) sit to the left with 8–12px spacing. The appearance of the bar pushes the black panel slightly downward, maintaining the framed margin of paper texture above and below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and disappears in the normal document flow, shifting the black panel downward when visible; it is not sticky and scrolls out of view with the header.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The url-state-sync component does not render any visible UI. It operates behind the scenes, aligning the current book detail state (active tab, selected sort order for reviews, open modals, etc.) with the URL. It therefore occupies no physical space in the layout and has no direct visual manifestation in the page’s structure.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual behavior; it does not affect spatial layout and has no scroll or position behavior.

name: Compare Books
title: Compare Books
description: Side-by-side comparison view for selected books, showing key attributes and allowing users to refine the compare set, add favorites, and open detailed book pages.

core_features: Load the active compare set and its books using getActiveCompareSetDetails, based on the optional compareSetId URL parameter.
Display each book in the compare set as a column in a comparison table with key Book attributes such as title, subtitle, format, price, rating, rating_count, page_count, and publication_date.
Calculate and highlight comparative metrics (for example, identifying which book has the highest or lowest page_count) purely from the loaded Book data.

Provide per-book actions: navigate to book_detail.html?id={book.id}, add the book to the default favorites list via addBookToFavorites, and remove the book from the compare set via removeBookFromCompareSet.
Provide compare-set level actions: clear all books from the current compare set using clearCompareSet, and navigate back to books.html to continue browsing.
Handle empty compare sets by showing an informational empty state and a navigation link back to books.html.
user_workflows: Compare books from the catalog: (1) User selects books to compare on books.html and opens the Compare Books page (optionally with a compareSetId in the URL). (2) On page load, the page reads the compareSetId URL parameter and calls getActiveCompareSetDetails. (3) The comparison table displays one column per book with key attributes. (4) User scans rows such as price, rating, and page_count to evaluate differences. (5) User optionally clicks 'Back to results' to return to books.html.

Identify longer or shorter books: (1) After the compare set is loaded via getActiveCompareSetDetails, the page computes the minimum and maximum page_count across the compared books. (2) The comparison table highlights the row for page_count and indicates which column(s) correspond to the longest and shortest books. (3) User uses this information to decide which book to explore in detail via 'View Details'.

Add a compared book to favorites: (1) In the comparison table, user clicks an 'Add to Favorites' action for a specific book column. (2) The page calls addBookToFavorites with that book's id. (3) On success, the component updates its local state to reflect that the book is in favorites and shows a success message based on the returned message. (4) On failure, the component shows an error message and allows the user to retry.

Refine or clear the compare set: (1) User clicks 'Remove from Compare' under a book column. (2) The page calls removeBookFromCompareSet with the corresponding compareSetItemId. (3) On success, the removed book column disappears from the comparison table, and the item count is updated. (4) If only one book remains, the table updates but still allows actions like 'View Details' and 'Add to Favorites'. (5) If user clicks 'Clear all', the page calls clearCompareSet; on successful response, the compare set becomes empty, the table hides, and the empty state with a link back to books.html is displayed.
interactions: Click 'View Details' for a book: navigates to book_detail.html with the URL parameter id set to that book's id; no API call is triggered on the Compare Books page for this interaction.

Click 'Add to Favorites' under a book: triggers addBookToFavorites(book.id); on success, the UI reflects the favorite state and shows a confirmation message; on failure, an error state is shown with the returned message.

Click 'Remove from Compare' under a book: triggers removeBookFromCompareSet(compareSetItemId); on success, the corresponding book column is removed from the comparison table and the item count is updated; on failure, the UI displays the returned message and leaves the column in place.

Click 'Clear all' in the compare actions bar: triggers clearCompareSet(); on success, the page state is updated so that there are no items, the comparison table is hidden, and the empty-state component is shown; on failure, the UI shows the returned message and keeps the current compare set visible.
Click 'Back to results' or 'Continue browsing': navigates to books.html without altering the compare set state on this page; the browser URL is updated accordingly.

Page load or URL change event: reads the compareSetId parameter (if present) from the current URL, then calls getActiveCompareSetDetails to load the appropriate active compare set and its items into page state before rendering the comparison table.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page derives its main state from a combination of the URL parameter compareSetId and the response of getActiveCompareSetDetails. On initial load and whenever the URL changes, the page reads compareSetId from the query string. The page then calls getActiveCompareSetDetails once to obtain the active CompareSet object and its associated items, including each related Book. The client assumes that, if compareSetId is present in the URL, the active compare set returned by getActiveCompareSetDetails corresponds to that ID (the server is responsible for honoring this contract). The compare set and items are stored in in-memory page state (not in localStorage). All subsequent user actions that change the compare set—removing an item or clearing the set—call removeBookFromCompareSet or clearCompareSet respectively. On successful responses, the page state is updated locally by removing the affected item(s) from the in-memory list and recalculating any derived metrics such as item count and page_count extrema. The URL parameter compareSetId remains unchanged during these operations, so the user can refresh the page and still refer to the same compare set context, even if it is now empty. Navigation to book_detail.html?id={book.id} and to books.html is handled purely through URL changes; no additional state is persisted beyond the current URL and the in-memory state built from the assigned interfaces.

components.functionality: Initializes the Compare Books page by reading the compareSetId URL parameter, calling getActiveCompareSetDetails, storing the returned compareSet and items in page state, and broadcasting loading and error status to other components.

Displays the current compare set context (such as the number of books being compared), provides a 'Clear all' action to empty the compare set using clearCompareSet, and provides a 'Back to results' or 'Continue browsing' link to navigate to books.html. Updates dynamically when books are removed or the set is cleared.

Renders a column-based comparison table where each column corresponds to a CompareSetItem and its associated Book, and each row corresponds to a specific attribute (e.g., title, subtitle, format, price, rating, rating_count, page_count, publication_date). It computes comparative metrics such as the minimum and maximum page_count across all visible books to highlight which book is longest or shortest and exposes hooks for child components to display labels or emphasis based on these calculations.

Instantiated once per book column in the comparison table, this component provides actions for that specific book: (1) 'View Details' navigates to book_detail.html?id={book.id}; (2) 'Add to Favorites' calls addBookToFavorites(book.id) and, on success, updates the local favorite state and displays feedback using the returned favoritesList, listItem, and message; (3) 'Remove from Compare' calls removeBookFromCompareSet(compareSetItemId) and, on success, notifies the parent comparison-table component to remove this column from the UI.

Displays when the active compare set has no items (either after loading or after clearing/removing all books). It shows an informational message indicating that no books are currently being compared and provides a single action link that navigates back to books.html so the user can select books to compare.

Centralized component for showing loading indicators and success or error messages for operations related to the assigned interfaces: getActiveCompareSetDetails, addBookToFavorites, removeBookFromCompareSet, and clearCompareSet. It listens to events from other components and renders corresponding status messages without storing any business data itself.

layout.grid_system: Fixed-width, center-aligned layout likely based on a 12-column grid, with a two-column main content area inside a full-width background.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the same top-to-bottom sequence as the mockup: a full-width site header and navigation on the textured paper background, then a centered black content panel framed by generous margins. Inside this panel, content is organized into a two-column layout. At the very top of the panel, the compare-page-init (title/intro) and compare-status-messages span the full width. Below that, the main content splits into two columns: the left column is a tall, stacked strip of book cards and actions (compare-book-actions), echoing the portrait panel from the mockup; the right column is a denser, information-heavy stack where the compare-summary-bar sits at the top and the compare-books-table fills the remaining space. When there are no books selected, the two-column structure collapses into a single centered compare-empty-state area inside the black panel.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the same top-to-bottom sequence as the mockup: a full-width site header and navigation on the textured paper background, then a centered black content panel framed by generous margins. Inside this panel, content is organized into a two-column layout. At the very top of the panel, the compare-page-init (title/intro) and compare-status-messages span the full width. Below that, the main content splits into two columns: the left column is a tall, stacked strip of book cards and actions (compare-book-actions), echoing the portrait panel from the mockup; the right column is a denser, information-heavy stack where the compare-summary-bar sits at the top and the compare-books-table fills the remaining space. When there are no books selected, the two-column structure collapses into a single centered compare-empty-state area inside the black panel.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header and nav occupy roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport height, with the black compare panel filling most of the remaining area but inset from the page edges by about 32–48px to maintain the framed, textured-paper border. Inside the black panel, about 40–45% of the width is allotted to the left column for the vertical book cards/actions, and about 55–60% to the right column for the summary bar and the comparison table. Within the right column, the compare-summary-bar claims the top 20–25% of the visible column height (or roughly 80–96px tall), with a small 16px gap below it; the compare-books-table occupies the rest of the vertical space. The compare-status-messages sit in a low-profile strip between the page-init and the two-column region, using just enough height (40–64px) to be readable without pushing content too far down. In the empty state, the black panel still uses the same margins, but the empty-state block occupies the central 60–70% of the panel width, centered both horizontally and vertically within the main content area below the title.
layout_strategies.content_density: The header remains relatively open and spacious, with comfortable vertical breathing room. Inside the black panel, the density increases in line with the mockup: the right column (summary and comparison table) is the most information-dense area, with tight vertical rhythm (8–12px between table rows, 16px between logical blocks). The left column of book cards/actions is moderately dense: cards are distinct with 16–24px internal padding and 16px gaps between them, but stacked closely enough to feel like a continuous strip of content. The status messages are slim and compact, and the summary bar is dense but visually unified into a single horizontal band. The empty state uses more generous spacing, with larger margins and more negative space around its icon/text/CTA, echoing the breathing room of the original portrait vs. form contrast.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup’s F-shaped pattern. The user’s eye first lands on the bold 'Compare Books' title and any brief description in the top of the black panel (compare-page-init). From there, the gaze moves horizontally across to the right side where the compare-summary-bar’s accented background and primary action buttons stand out. The eye then drops down to scan the left column of tall book cards (compare-book-actions), which visually echo the portrait area from the original design. From these cards, the gaze is pulled across to the right into the denser comparison table, where the user reads row-by-row down the page. Status messages briefly intercept the flow just under the title without overwhelming it, and in the empty state, the eye goes from the title directly down to the centered empty-state content and its main call-to-action.

layout.overall_description:
The Compare Books page sits on the same dark, dramatic author portfolio canvas as the rest of the site: a full-width crumpled-paper background with an inky black header at the top and a centered black content panel below. The header and navigation stretch across the top, with the current 'Compare' or 'Books' section highlighted in red to anchor the page context. Below this, the main black panel is inset from the edges of the viewport, creating a framed window of content surrounded by the textured paper. Inside the panel, the top band is simple and strong: on the left, the 'Compare Books' title and a brief line of explanatory copy are left-aligned in white text; on the right, there may be a subtle count of how many books are currently selected. Just beneath this, any status messages appear as slim, colored strips that cut horizontally across the full width of the panel without dominating it. 

The main body of the panel is split into two asymmetrical columns. On the left, occupying about two-fifths of the width, a column of dark book cards runs downward, each with a cover image, title, and key actions like 'Favorite,' 'Remove from Compare,' and 'View Details.' This column feels like a stack of character portraits: tall, visually rich, and strongly bordered, mirroring the portrait emphasis in the original design. On the right, taking up slightly more than half the width, the content becomes denser and more utilitarian. At the top of this right column, a horizontally banded compare-summary-bar—with a slightly different dark tone or subtle red accent—summarizes what is being compared and provides controls for refining the comparison (adding books, clearing the compare list, adjusting filters). Immediately below this bar, separated by a narrow gap, the comparison table fills the remaining height, scrolling as needed. The table presents each selected book as a column and each attribute as a row, with strong vertical and horizontal rules, crisp typography, and compact row spacing to underscore the analytical, side-by-side nature of the view. If no books are selected, this two-column system is temporarily replaced by a single, centered empty state module within the black panel, framed by generous whitespace and a clear call-to-action to start selecting books. Across the whole layout, the eye moves from the bold title, across the action bar, down through the visual stack of book cards, and then across and down through the comparison table, preserving the moody, literary, but functional aesthetic of the original mockup.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The compare-page-init component occupies the topmost content band inside the black central panel, directly beneath the full-width site header and navigation. It spans the full width of the black panel with left and right padding of around 24px (3 × 8px base units) and top/bottom padding of 16–24px. The main 'Compare Books' heading is left-aligned, sitting roughly one-third of the way down from the top edge of the panel’s internal padding, with a brief, single-line description positioned immediately below it with 8–12px vertical spacing. If a count of selected books is displayed, it is aligned to the right edge of this band, vertically centered with the main heading line, creating a subtle left-right tension. Visually, this component acts as a calm, spacious introduction before the denser two-column layout begins about 16–24px below it. The bottom of this band aligns with the top of the status messages (if present), ensuring that the title area feels like a unified header for the compare experience.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the top of the black panel; it scrolls away with the rest of the page content and does not stick or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The compare-status-messages component sits immediately below the compare-page-init band, sharing the same overall width as the black panel. It presents one or more slim horizontal alert strips, each spanning approximately 90–100% of the internal width of the panel and inset from the sides by the same 24px padding as the title area, so the edges line up cleanly. Each message bar is about 40–56px tall, with 8–12px vertical spacing between stacked messages when multiple are present. The bars are aligned flush-left with the title text above and terminate just before the right internal padding, maintaining a strong column. A vertical gap of around 16px separates the bottom of the last status message from the start of the main two-column content below. These messages hover visually between the calm title area and the busier content area, catching the eye as it moves down the page but remaining compact enough not to break the overall flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, appearing and disappearing in place beneath the title; when present they push the main two-column content downward but do not overlay it or stick on scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The compare-summary-bar is anchored at the top of the right-hand column within the black panel’s main two-column region. Horizontally, this right column occupies roughly 55–60% of the black panel’s width and is aligned to the inner right edge of the panel. The summary bar spans the full width of this right column, with its left edge vertically aligned to the centerline where the two columns meet. It sits about 16–24px below the bottom of the status-messages area (or the page-init, if there are no messages). The bar itself is a horizontal band approximately 80–96px tall, with 16px of internal padding on all sides. Inside, summary text (e.g., 'You’re comparing 3 books') is left-aligned within the bar, while a cluster of compact buttons—'Add Book,' 'Clear All,' maybe a filter toggle—are right-aligned, forming a horizontal hierarchy that echoes the navigation in the header. A strong contrasting background (a deeper black or dark red) and a thin border emphasize its importance and visually echo the subscription box from the original mockup. The bottom of the bar is separated from the comparison table below by a narrow 8–16px gap, making it feel connected yet distinct.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves like a local sticky header for the right column on larger screens: as the user scrolls the page, the summary bar can remain pinned to the top edge of the right column area while the comparison table scrolls beneath it, ensuring that actions are always accessible. On small screens or when sticky is not possible, it scrolls normally with the content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The compare-books-table occupies the majority of the right-hand column below the compare-summary-bar. It shares the same right-column width (about 55–60% of the black panel) and starts roughly 8–16px beneath the bottom edge of the summary bar. The table stretches from this point down toward the bottom padding of the black panel, with about 24px of bottom padding before the panel’s edge. Horizontally, the table content is left-aligned with the summary bar above, with a thin internal margin (8–16px) between the table’s outermost grid lines and the right column’s padding. Each book appears as a vertical column within the table, and the first column on the left lists attribute names, reinforcing the side-by-side comparison feel. Rows are tight and information-dense, with 8–12px vertical padding, and strong but narrow rules between rows and columns reflect the structured, form-like density of the mockup’s contact form. The table’s header row, containing book titles or cover thumbnails, sits at the top of the table body and reads horizontally; this corresponds visually to the 'Send a Message' label and top form row in the original design. There is a clear visual alignment between the right edge of the left column (book cards) and the left edge of this table, so the eye can easily travel from a card on the left into its corresponding column on the right. On scroll, the table extends downward, becoming the primary area of vertical exploration within the panel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The table itself scrolls with the page; on larger viewports, the header row can be made sticky within the table area so that book names remain visible as the user scrolls down through attributes. The table does not float or overlap the left column; both columns scroll together as a unified panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The compare-book-actions component forms the entire left column of the two-column layout inside the black panel’s main content area. Starting at the same vertical position as the compare-summary-bar on the right, this column sits directly under the status messages (or under the page-init if there are no messages), offset downward by 16–24px. It occupies approximately 40–45% of the panel’s width and is flush with the panel’s left internal padding. Within this column, individual book cards are stacked vertically, each card representing one compared book. Cards are tall, portrait-oriented blocks roughly 120–160px in height, with a 16–24px internal padding and a 16px gap between cards, echoing the tall portrait card from the mockup. Each card features a small cover image aligned to the left, book title and key metadata to the right, and a row of controls—'Remove,' 'Favorite,' 'Open Details'—anchored at the bottom or right edge of the card. The card’s outer border and slight drop shadow lift it subtly off the black panel, making this column visually rich and character-driven compared to the more utilitarian right column. The bottom of the last card sits above the panel’s bottom padding by around 24px. The left edge of this column aligns with the start of the panel’s content, and its right edge aligns precisely with the left edge of the comparison table, preserving a strong central vertical dividing line between the two functional zones.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and scrolls with the rest of the panel. The entire column moves as a unit; cards do not float independently. On narrower screens, this column can collapse to sit above the comparison table in a single-column stack while maintaining internal card spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The compare-empty-state component takes over the central content area of the black panel whenever there are no books selected for comparison. In this state, the usual two-column layout (compare-book-actions on the left and compare-books-table with summary bar on the right) is suppressed or hidden. The empty-state block sits below the compare-page-init and any status messages, starting about 32–40px underneath them to emphasize a pause in the normal flow. It is centered horizontally within the black panel and spans roughly 60–70% of the panel’s width, leaving generous margins of textured black space on either side. Vertically, it is balanced so that its center aligns roughly with the midpoint of the black panel’s height when possible, creating a calm focal area. Inside this block, an icon or illustrative mark sits at the top, followed by a bold heading (e.g., 'No books to compare yet') and a short line of guidance text, each separated by about 8–12px. Below that, a primary call-to-action button—such as 'Browse Books'—is centered and visually emphasized, using the same red or orange accent as other CTAs on the site. The internal padding of the empty-state card is generous (24–32px), echoing the breathing room seen around the portrait area in the mockup and contrasting with the denser comparison table normally displayed.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditional and exclusive: it only appears when there are zero selected books and in that state occupies the main body of the black panel beneath the title, pushing out or replacing the two-column content. It scrolls normally with the page if content above it grows, but on typical viewports it appears visually centered without sticking.

name: Bundle Summary
title: Bundle Summary
description: Review and manage the contents of an eBook bundle, adjust items and quantities, and add the bundle to the shopping cart.

core_features: Load the active eBook bundle (or the bundle specified by the URL parameter) and display all included books, quantities, and line totals using getActiveBundleDetails.
Allow users to change the quantity for each bundle item and immediately update bundle totals using updateBundleItemQuantity.
Allow users to remove individual books from the bundle and update bundle totals using removeBundleItem.
Display a bundle pricing summary (item count and total price) that stays in sync with updates and removals.
Provide a primary action to add the current bundle as a single item to the shopping cart using addBundleToCart, then navigate to cart.html with URL parameters.
Provide navigation back to the sci-fi eBook catalog (books.html) with appropriate URL parameters to continue browsing and adding more books.
user_workflows: Reviewing the active bundle: (1) User opens bundle.html, optionally with a bundleId query parameter in the URL. (2) The page initialization logic parses bundleId from the URL but always calls getActiveBundleDetails without exposing internal implementation details. (3) The bundle items list displays each book in the bundle, including quantity and line total, along with overall bundle totals in a summary panel. (4) If the bundle has no items, an empty-state message is shown with a link to continue browsing on books.html.

Adjusting item quantities: (1) User interacts with a quantity control for a specific bundle item (e.g., changes the numeric value or clicks increase/decrease controls). (2) The quantity control component validates the requested quantity locally (e.g., non-negative, integer) before calling updateBundleItemQuantity with the bundleItemId and the new quantity. (3) While the request is in progress, the control enters a loading state to prevent overlapping updates. (4) On success, the component reads bundleTotals from the response and updates the summary panel. (5) To keep line totals and any pricing rules accurate, the page re-calls getActiveBundleDetails and refreshes the visible list and line totals. (6) On error, the component reverts to the previous quantity and shows an error message via the notification component.

Removing items from the bundle: (1) User clicks a Remove action on a specific bundle item row. (2) The row triggers removeBundleItem with the corresponding bundleItemId. (3) While waiting for the response, the row visually indicates a pending removal and disables further interaction. (4) On success, the component uses the returned bundleTotals to update the summary panel and either removes the item from local state or re-calls getActiveBundleDetails to refresh the list. (5) If the resulting item count is zero, the page switches to the empty-bundle state and disables the Add Bundle to Cart button. (6) On error, the row remains visible and an error message is displayed via the notification component.

Adding the bundle to the cart: (1) After reviewing and adjusting items, user clicks the Add Bundle to Cart button in the summary panel. (2) The button triggers addBundleToCart with the bundleId obtained from the bundle object returned by getActiveBundleDetails. (3) While the request is in progress, the button is disabled and shows a loading state to prevent duplicate submissions. (4) On success, the component reads cartSummary and cartItemId from the response and optionally displays a short confirmation message. (5) The page then navigates to cart.html with the URL parameter from=bundle so the cart page can recognize the source of navigation. (6) On error, the button returns to an enabled state and a descriptive error message from the response is shown via the notification component; the user remains on bundle.html to retry or adjust items.

Continuing to browse sci-fi eBooks: (1) From either the bundle items list or the empty-bundle state, user clicks a Continue browsing / Add more sci-fi eBooks navigation link. (2) The link navigates to books.html with the URL parameters genre=science_fiction and format=ebook, without invoking any bundle-specific interface. (3) When the user returns to the bundle summary page (via site navigation or a dedicated 'View Bundle' link), bundle.html loads again, and getActiveBundleDetails is called to show the current in-progress bundle.
interactions: On page load or when the URL changes, the page initialization component reads the bundleId query parameter (if present) and calls getActiveBundleDetails to fetch the active bundle context, items, and totals.

When the user edits a quantity input for a bundle item and confirms the change (e.g., leaving the field or pressing an Apply control), the quantity control calls updateBundleItemQuantity with the bundleItemId bound to that row and the new quantity value from the input.

When the user clicks an increment or decrement control next to a quantity field, the quantity control adjusts the displayed value, validates it, and then calls updateBundleItemQuantity with the derived quantity.
When the user clicks the Remove button on a bundle item row, the row component calls removeBundleItem with the associated bundleItemId and updates UI state based on the success or error response.

After successful quantity updates or item removals, the list and summary components either update their local state using the new totals or trigger a fresh call to getActiveBundleDetails to ensure all prices and line totals reflect server-side logic.

When the user clicks the Add Bundle to Cart button in the summary panel, the button component calls addBundleToCart with the current bundle.id, then on success navigates to cart.html with the query parameter from=bundle.

When the user clicks the Continue browsing link, the navigation component performs a client-side redirect to books.html with URL parameters genre=science_fiction and format=ebook, without interacting with any of the bundle interfaces.

When any interface call (getActiveBundleDetails, updateBundleItemQuantity, removeBundleItem, addBundleToCart) returns an error or a message field, the notification component displays this message to the user and provides a way to dismiss it, without altering the bundle state unless explicitly required.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page state is primarily driven by URL parameters and the responses from the assigned interfaces. The key URL parameter is bundleId, which is optional. On initialization, the page reads bundleId from the query string of bundle.html. This value is not passed directly to getActiveBundleDetails (since the interface does not accept parameters), but is assumed to inform back-end selection of the active bundle via server-side mechanisms; the front-end's responsibility is to maintain bundleId in the URL so the same bundle can be revisited or shared. Regardless of whether bundleId is present, the page always calls getActiveBundleDetails to populate the current bundle, its items, and totals. The bundle object returned from getActiveBundleDetails provides a canonical bundle.id, which is used for subsequent calls to addBundleToCart. When navigating away from the Bundle Summary page, URL parameters are used to preserve context: cart.html is opened as cart.html?from=bundle after a successful addBundleToCart call, and books.html is opened as books.html?genre=science_fiction&format=ebook when the user chooses to continue browsing sci-fi eBooks. The page maintains internal UI states such as loading (while getActiveBundleDetails is in progress), updating-item (while updateBundleItemQuantity or removeBundleItem is in progress for a specific row), adding-to-cart (while addBundleToCart is in progress), error (when any interface call fails), and empty-bundle (when the item list is empty according to getActiveBundleDetails or a subsequent successful removal). These states are derived solely from live interface responses and are not persisted via localStorage or any other client-side storage; the URL parameters and server responses are the single sources of truth for which bundle is active and what its contents and totals are.

components.functionality: Initializes the Bundle Summary page by parsing the bundleId query parameter from the page URL, calling getActiveBundleDetails to load the active bundle, and populating shared state with the bundle object, its item list, and initial totals. Manages global loading and error states for the page.

Displays all books currently included in the bundle. For each item, renders a bundle-item-row with book information, the item quantity, line total, and controls for changing quantity or removing the item. Handles the transition between non-empty and empty states based on the items returned by getActiveBundleDetails or after updates/removals.

Represents a single bundle item row. Binds to a specific item from the items array returned by getActiveBundleDetails, showing the associated book details (via the embedded Book object), the current quantity, and the line_total. Embeds the quantity control and remove button and propagates their results to the parent list and summary components.

Provides the user interface for adjusting the quantity of a single bundle item. Reads the current quantity from its bound item and allows the user to increase, decrease, or directly edit the numeric value. On confirmed changes, calls updateBundleItemQuantity with the associated bundleItemId and new quantity, then updates the bundle summary totals and triggers a list refresh via getActiveBundleDetails if needed.

Removes a specific book from the bundle. When clicked, calls removeBundleItem with the bound bundleItemId, then updates the bundle totals using the returned bundleTotals and instructs the items list to remove the item from view or to refresh via getActiveBundleDetails. Handles disabled/loading state while the removal is in progress.

Shows the overall bundle summary, including item count and total_price with the correct currency. Initializes its values from getActiveBundleDetails and keeps them in sync using bundleTotals returned from updateBundleItemQuantity and removeBundleItem. Contains the primary Add Bundle to Cart button, which is disabled when there are no items or while an add-to-cart request is in progress.

Handles adding the complete bundle to the shopping cart. Uses the bundle.id from the current bundle state (populated by getActiveBundleDetails) to call addBundleToCart. On success, it may surface feedback using the returned message and cartSummary, then navigates to cart.html with the URL parameter from=bundle.

Provides a link for users to continue browsing sci-fi eBooks to add to the bundle. When activated, navigates to books.html with the query parameters genre=science_fiction and format=ebook. It does not call any bundle interfaces but relies on navigation for further bundle modifications.

Displays an informative empty state when the bundle has no items according to getActiveBundleDetails or after successful removals. Hides the items list, disables or hides the Add Bundle to Cart button, and prominently shows the Continue browsing link to books.html.

Collects and displays user-visible messages from the bundle interfaces, including success and error messages from updateBundleItemQuantity, removeBundleItem, addBundleToCart, and failures of getActiveBundleDetails. Provides dismiss controls that clear the currently displayed message state without modifying bundle data.

layout.grid_system: Fixed-width, center-aligned layout likely based on a 12-column grid, with a two-column main content area inside a full-width background.
layout.layout_pattern: Maintain the mockup’s structure: a full-width dark header and navigation at the very top, with the current section ('Bundle Summary') highlighted in red. Below, a large centered black panel sits on the crumpled paper background. Inside that panel, use a two-column layout: the left column is a tall, scrollable bundle items list (replacing the author portrait), and the right column stacks the bundle summary panel with totals and primary actions (replacing the subscription + contact form area). Cross-cutting elements like feedback messages sit at the top of the panel spanning both columns, while secondary navigation like 'Continue Browsing' sits near the bottom, aligned with the content grid.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Maintain the mockup’s structure: a full-width dark header and navigation at the very top, with the current section ('Bundle Summary') highlighted in red. Below, a large centered black panel sits on the crumpled paper background. Inside that panel, use a two-column layout: the left column is a tall, scrollable bundle items list (replacing the author portrait), and the right column stacks the bundle summary panel with totals and primary actions (replacing the subscription + contact form area). Cross-cutting elements like feedback messages sit at the top of the panel spanning both columns, while secondary navigation like 'Continue Browsing' sits near the bottom, aligned with the content grid.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header consumes roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport. The central black panel is inset from the page edges with 32–48px of textured paper margin on all sides and occupies the remaining 80–85% of viewport height. Inside the panel, the left column (bundle items) takes about 45% of the panel width; the right column (summary and actions) takes about 55%. Vertical space in the right column is divided so that the summary panel (totals, meta info) takes the top ~60–70% of the column’s height, with the primary 'Add to Cart' button anchored toward the lower part of that panel and any supporting links or notes beneath. The bottom band of the panel (~10–15% of height) visually supports secondary actions like 'Continue Browsing.'
layout_strategies.content_density: The header is relatively open and airy with clear hierarchy. The central black panel is medium-to-high density: the bundle items list packs rows with modest vertical gaps (8–12px between items), while the summary panel uses compact stacking of text, totals, and controls with 8–16px vertical spacing. Padding inside cards and panels follows the 16–24px standard. This density deliberately contrasts with the surrounding textured paper margin, making the central content feel focused and functional while still moody and dramatic.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual entry starts at the bright, centered author/brand name and bundle page title in the header, then moves horizontally across the navigation where 'BUNDLE' or 'SHOP' is highlighted in red. The eye then drops into the black panel, first catching any feedback messages at the very top, then falling down the left column through the vertically stacked bundle items. From there, attention is pulled right toward the higher-contrast summary panel and its primary 'Add to Cart' button (accent color similar to the mockup’s orange). Finally, the gaze moves downward along the right edge to any secondary links and to the 'Continue Browsing' link, mirroring the original F-shaped reading path.

layout.overall_description: The page sits on a textured, off-white paper background with a dramatic black header bar stretching full width across the top. Centered in the header is the author’s name and site title in strong serif typography, flanked or underlined by a thin red accent line, with navigation items aligned in a row and the current commerce section accented in red. Beneath this, a large, centered black content panel occupies most of the remaining viewport, leaving a generous paper frame around it. Inside this panel, a subtle 12-column grid divides content into two primary columns. On the left, a tall, scrollable list of bundle items runs from just below a slim feedback area almost to the bottom of the panel, each item row outlined with faint borders and lit by white text and red highlights. On the right, occupying slightly more width, a structured summary block sits near the top: a dark card with a bold 'Bundle Summary' heading, line items for subtotal, discounts, and final price, and a large, high-contrast 'Add to Cart' button aligned toward the lower right of the card. Any feedback messages appear as a band across the top edge of the black panel, tying both columns together. If the bundle is empty, a centered empty-state message replaces the left list, still confined to the left column region, maintaining the two-column rhythm. At the bottom of the black panel, aligned to the left or lower-left of the grid, a 'Continue Browsing' text link offers a gentle escape route. Throughout, tight vertical spacing, crisp borders, and strong red and orange accents on buttons create a moody, literary shopping interface that clearly guides the user’s eye from bundle contents to summary and finally to the primary purchase action.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data-loader component operates as an overlay within the central black panel. When active, it dims the entire panel’s content (both left items column and right summary column) with a semi-transparent black veil and centers a small loading indicator (spinner or progress glyph) both vertically and horizontally in the panel. The overlay matches the panel’s width (about 70–80% of the page width) and height (the full height of the central panel), respecting the 32–48px paper margin around it. The loader itself is compact—roughly 64–96px in diameter—with a subtle red or white accent ring that echoes the site’s dramatic styling, leaving ample dark space around it so the user’s attention is drawn to the loading state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily positioned as a fixed overlay within the central black panel (not the whole viewport), centered both horizontally and vertically; blocks interaction with underlying components while active and disappears to reveal the standard two-column layout once data is loaded.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The bundle items list anchors the left column inside the central black panel. It begins just below any feedback messages, about 24–32px from the top edge of the panel, and extends downward almost to the bottom padding of the panel, leaving a 24–32px gap at the bottom. The list occupies roughly 45% of the panel’s width and is aligned with the left inner edge of the panel’s grid, with 16–24px of internal padding from the panel border. Items are stacked vertically with 8–12px gaps between rows, producing a compact but readable list. The list area can scroll independently if there are many items, while the right summary column remains in view. A subtle vertical dividing line or a strong contrast gap (16–24px gutter) separates this left list from the right summary column, reinforcing the two-column structure seen in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Occupies a fixed left-side column within the black panel. The container itself follows the page scroll, but its internal content may scroll independently if item count exceeds available vertical space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each bundle item row appears as a horizontally oriented band within the left column, spanning the full width of the bundle-items-list container minus 16–24px of internal padding on each side. Rows are approximately 56–72px tall, depending on content, and are separated by 8–12px of vertical space. The left side of the row (about 60–65% of the row width) contains the book thumbnail or title and brief metadata, aligned to the left with white text on the black background. The right side of the row (about 35–40% of the row width) is reserved for controls—quantity control aligned to the upper-right quadrant and the remove button aligned to the far right edge. Optional faint bottom borders or subtle dark gray separators echo the mockup’s structured card feel, keeping content dense but visually bounded.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows vertically within the bundle-items-list container; rows compress or expand slightly in height based on content but maintain consistent alignment of controls to the right, preserving a clean columnar rhythm.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The quantity control sits inside the bundle-item-row towards the right, roughly aligned to the vertical center of the row. It occupies a compact horizontal block (about 25–30% of the item row width) with a plus-minus stepper or numeric input, with 8px internal padding and 4–8px gaps between its internal elements. The control is anchored about 8–12px from the right edge of the text area and 8–12px from the right edge of the row content, leaving enough space for the remove button to sit flush at the far right. The control’s styling uses a dark input field with a fine light border and red or white accents for active states, keeping it visually integrated into the moody aesthetic but clearly interactive.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed position within each item row’s layout grid, aligned to the right side; does not float or overlap. The control size remains consistent across rows, ensuring a clean vertical alignment of quantity inputs down the column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The remove item button is positioned at the far right end of each bundle-item-row, vertically centered. It is a small, high-contrast text or icon button (e.g., 'Remove' or an '×'), occupying about 10–15% of the row width. There is a minimal horizontal gap (4–8px) between the quantity control and the remove button, maintaining the compact feel. The button aligns flush with the right padding of the bundle-items-list, so all remove buttons form a crisp vertical line down the right edge of the left column. The button uses a red accent color on hover or focus to echo the mockup’s use of red for critical actions, but default state may be a subdued light gray to avoid overpowering the primary 'Add to Cart' CTA.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline with the content of each item row, anchored to the row’s right edge; participates in normal layout flow and does not stick or float across rows.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The summary panel occupies the upper portion of the right column within the central black panel. It begins at the same top baseline as the bundle-items-list (just below any feedback messages), aligned to the right inner edge of the panel with 16–24px of padding to the panel border. The panel spans the full width of the right column (about 55% of the panel’s width) and has 16–24px internal padding on all sides, creating a distinct card-like feel on the black background. Inside, content is stacked vertically: a bold 'Bundle Summary' heading at the top, followed by line items for number of books, subtotal, discounts, and final total, each separated by 8–12px. A subtle divider line can separate informational rows from the action area below. The lower third of the panel is reserved for the primary action region, where the 'Add to Cart' button is aligned to the bottom-right corner of the panel, with a small note or secondary text (e.g., delivery info) aligned left in that same lower band. The panel height is sufficient to feel substantial—roughly 60–70% of the right column height—mirroring the stacked form area in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed position in the right column, scrolling with the page as a unit. Its vertical size is content-driven but designed to visually balance with the left column’s list height; does not behave as sticky by default.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'Add to Cart' button is the focal action and is placed inside the bundle-summary-panel, anchored to the lower-right corner of that panel. It spans roughly 60–70% of the summary panel’s width, with a minimum width of about 200–240px, and is about 40–48px tall. There is 16px of space above it and 8–16px of space below it to the bottom edge of the panel, keeping it visually grounded but not cramped. The button aligns with the right edge of the panel’s content area, creating a strong vertical line with the right column and echoing the bottom-right 'Send' button placement from the mockup. It uses a vivid accent color (e.g., orange or intense red) against the black, with bold white text, making it the brightest element in the panel and a key endpoint in the visual flow.
