layout.layout_pattern: The page retains the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure: a full-width dark header with the author’s name and global navigation at the very top (outside the scope of these components), followed by a centered, fixed-width black content panel that holds all About page content. Inside this black panel, the layout uses a two-column structure: the left column is dedicated to the author-profile-section as a tall, visually dominant profile card; the right column stacks the about-page-navigation-links module at the top and the notable-books-list below it, mirroring the newsletter + form stack from the original mockup. The about-page-controller runs behind or above these, driving state and optionally showing loading states over the main panel.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page retains the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure: a full-width dark header with the author’s name and global navigation at the very top (outside the scope of these components), followed by a centered, fixed-width black content panel that holds all About page content. Inside this black panel, the layout uses a two-column structure: the left column is dedicated to the author-profile-section as a tall, visually dominant profile card; the right column stacks the about-page-navigation-links module at the top and the notable-books-list below it, mirroring the newsletter + form stack from the original mockup. The about-page-controller runs behind or above these, driving state and optionally showing loading states over the main panel.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header still occupies roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport height. The central black panel below it spans about 70–80% of the viewport width, centered, leaving a visible frame of textured paper around it (roughly 32–48px margins on left and right). Inside the panel, the left author profile column takes about 40–45% of the panel width; the right column takes about 55–60%. Vertically in the right column, the navigation-links module sits in the top 25–30% (similar in depth to the original subscription box), with the notable-books-list filling the remaining 70–75% of that column’s height. Internal padding within the black panel and its submodules uses 16–24px, consistent with the base spacing scale.
layout_strategies.content_density: The black content panel follows the mockup’s medium-to-high content density: text blocks, links, and book entries are stacked with compact vertical gaps of 8–12px and section separations of 16–24px. The author profile card combines a large portrait and tightly set typographic content (bio, accolades) with relatively small spacing between lines and subheadings, echoing the dense feel of the form fields in the mockup. The right column is more information-dense, with navigation links and book items listed closely together, while the outer header and the whitespace around the black panel remain more spacious.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual path follows an F-shaped pattern adapted from the mockup. The eye first lands on the bright author name and primary navigation in the full-width header, where the ABOUT item is highlighted (analogous to the red CONTACT state). From there, attention drops into the main black panel: it first catches the large author portrait and profile on the left. The viewer’s gaze is then pulled horizontally to the right column, starting with the highlighted navigation-links module (acting like the original Subscribe box), then moves downward through the notable-books-list entries. Secondary horizontal scans occur as users read across each book entry or navigation link, reinforcing the F-shape: top horizontal bar (header), mid-horizontal (profile to nav links), and lower vertical scan down through the list of books.

layout.overall_description: The About page sits on a textured, crumpled-paper background with a full-width dark header bar at the top carrying the author’s name, tagline, and main navigation; the ABOUT item is marked in red to signal the active page. Below this, a large, centered black panel occupies the central 70–80% of the viewport width and most of its height, inset from the page edges by a generous frame of textured paper. Within this black panel, the layout splits into two columns. On the left, a tall author profile section runs from near the top padding of the panel almost to its bottom, dominated by a large portrait aligned toward the upper half and a structured biography and credentials arranged beneath. On the right, a stacked set of content begins with a compact but visually prominent navigation box that offers deep links into the books catalog, individual book details, and the blog, styled like a call-to-explore module. Below this, filling most of the right-hand column, a notable-books list presents featured titles in a dense vertical sequence, each with title, cover thumbnail, and a short description. The spacing between elements inside the panel is tight but orderly, creating a sense of intellectual intensity and narrative richness, while the surrounding paper texture and the broad header above keep the layout feeling framed and intentional. The page-controller component quietly orchestrates data loading and can temporarily overlay skeleton states over these zones, but visually the experience is defined by the strong two-column composition, rich typography, and dramatic contrast between black content areas and the paper background.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-page-controller is not a visible content block in the normal state; it sits logically above the main black panel in the component hierarchy and controls loading and error states for the author-profile-section, notable-books-list, and about-page-navigation-links. When data is loading, it may render a semi-transparent dark overlay over the entire black content panel (from the panel’s top padding down to its bottom, spanning its full width) with a centered, narrow loading indicator at approximately the vertical middle of the panel. This overlay respects the same panel bounds: it does not extend into the textured paper margin or the global header; instead, it creates a subtle veil over the two-column content area. Any skeleton placeholders it renders (for instance, greyed-out rectangles for the portrait, text lines, or list items) follow the same two-column proportions as the final content: a wider left skeleton block covering about 40–45% for the profile and stacked horizontal skeleton rows on the right for navigation and books.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as an overlay layer within the bounds of the central black panel, absolutely positioned relative to that panel. It appears and disappears based on loading state, without affecting scroll position. In non-loading states, it is effectively invisible and takes no visible space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Inside the central black panel, the author-profile-section is anchored to the left side and occupies approximately 40–45% of the panel’s internal width. It starts just below the panel’s top padding (16–24px) and extends vertically almost to the panel’s bottom padding, forming a tall, card-like column. The section has internal padding of about 24px, with its content arranged top-to-bottom: at the very top, a large, vertically oriented author portrait spans roughly the upper 40–50% of this column’s height, centered horizontally within the left column but aligned close to the top to echo the tall portrait card from the mockup. Directly beneath the portrait, the author’s name is displayed prominently in bold, high-contrast typography, followed by a short tagline or role line, then a series of compact paragraphs detailing biography, career highlights, and thematic interests. These text elements are stacked with tight vertical spacing (8–12px between lines and 16px between logical groups like bio vs. accolades) to create a dense, narrative-heavy block. The right edge of this section aligns visually with the boundary between the two columns, creating a clear vertical division, while the left edge aligns with the panel’s inner padding, maintaining a consistent margin with the panel border and preserving the framed effect.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block within the main scroll flow, locked to the left column. On taller viewports, it may leave a small amount of breathing space below its content before the panel’s bottom padding; on shorter viewports, its content becomes scrollable along with the rest of the page, maintaining its full height in proportion to the right column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The notable-books-list sits in the right column of the central black panel and occupies the lower portion of that column, directly beneath the about-page-navigation-links. It spans the full width of the right column (approximately 55–60% of the panel’s internal width) and begins after a modest vertical gap of about 16px following the navigation-links module, mirroring the spacing between the subscription box and form in the original mockup. The section’s top aligns roughly with the mid-height of the left author-profile-section, and its content flows downward to near the bottom padding of the panel. Inside, book entries are arranged in a dense vertical list. Each entry typically uses a two-column micro-layout: on the left side of each entry (about 25–30% of the entry width), a small cover thumbnail or emblem is aligned to the top; to the right (70–75% of the entry width), the book title is set in bold red or high-contrast white text, with a short logline or description beneath in smaller type. Entries are separated by subtle separators or 8–12px vertical gaps to keep the list readable but compact. The overall effect is a tight stack of notable works that visually balances the height and weight of the author profile on the left, creating an F-pattern continuation as users scan down the right column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the scrollable page, filling available vertical space under the navigation-links module. On viewports where the list is taller than the visible area, it scrolls with the page; it does not use independent scrolling to preserve a cohesive column experience.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The about-page-navigation-links component is positioned in the top region of the right column, acting as a compact, high-visibility module analogous to the newsletter subscription box in the mockup. It is aligned with the top of the author-profile-section, starting just below the black panel’s top padding, and spans the full width of the right column (approximately 55–60% of the panel’s internal width). Vertically, it occupies roughly 25–30% of the right column’s height or enough space for a heading and 3–4 prominent links. The module has its own internal padding of about 16–24px and is framed by a subtle border or elevated background tone to set it apart from the books list beneath. At the top of this module, a bold heading such as “Explore More” appears, followed by vertically stacked navigation links: ‘Books Catalog’, ‘Featured Book’, ‘Blog’, etc. Each link is given a button-like or highlighted text treatment, with the currently relevant destination (for example, ‘Books Catalog’) possibly accented in a warm color (orange or red) similar to the subscribe button in the mockup, drawing the eye across from the left-column portrait. Vertical spacing between links is tight (8–12px), maintaining the medium-to-high density. The bottom edge of this module sits just above the notable-books-list, separated by a modest gap to clearly signal the transition from navigation to content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static component at the top of the right column, participating in the normal scroll flow. It does not stick or float; instead, it anchors the top of the right column and sets the visual entry point for that side of the layout.

name: FAQ
title: FAQ
description: Page for viewing and filtering frequently asked questions about books, formats, lists, bundles, events, and ordering, with direct navigation to related pages like Books, Events, and Contact.

core_features: Load all FAQ entries from the getFaqEntries interface when the page initializes.
Apply an optional category filter based on the URL parameter `category`, so users can land directly on a specific FAQ topic.
Allow users to change the active FAQ category from within the page, updating the `category` URL parameter without requiring a full reload.
Provide a keyword search input that filters the currently loaded FAQ entries client-side by question and answer text.
Display FAQ entries in an expandable/collapsible list, so users can click a question to reveal or hide its answer.
Generate a shareable link for each FAQ entry (using the entry’s `id`), so users can deep-link to a specific question.
Recognize and support internal links in answers that navigate to books.html, events.html, and contact.html with the correct URL parameters (e.g., bulk order inquiries).
user_workflows:
Browse all FAQs without filters:
1) User navigates to faq.html without any URL parameters.
2) The page calls getFaqEntries and receives an array of FAQ entries.
3) The page derives the set of available categories from the returned entries.
4) With no `category` parameter present, all entries are considered in scope, and the category filter shows an 'all' state.
5) The user scrolls through the list of questions; each question can be expanded/collapsed to reveal its answer.
6) If an answer contains links (e.g., to books.html or events.html), clicking them navigates the user to those pages in a new URL.

Arrive with a category pre-selected (deep link):
1) User clicks a link on another page (e.g., index.html) that points to faq.html?category=bulk_orders (or another category value).
2) The FAQ page reads the `category` URL parameter on load.
3) The page calls getFaqEntries and receives all entries.
4) After data loads, the page applies a client-side filter to show only entries whose `category` exactly matches the URL parameter value.
5) The category filter UI highlights the matching category as active.
6) The user can click other categories to change the filter, which updates the `category` URL parameter accordingly.

Use search to find a specific answer:
1) User is on faq.html, with or without a `category` filter.
2) The page has already loaded entries via getFaqEntries.
3) The user types into the search input and submits the form (or updates live as they type, depending on implementation).
4) The page filters the in-memory FAQ entries by matching the search text against each entry’s `question` and `answer` fields.
5) The FAQ list updates to show only matching questions, still constrained by the current `category` filter (if any).
6) The user clears the search to see all entries for the current category again.

Change category from within the FAQ page:
1) User arrives on faq.html (with or without `category` in the URL) and the page loads entries via getFaqEntries.
2) The category filter component displays all distinct categories derived from the loaded entries, plus an 'all' option.
3) The user clicks on a category option.
4) The page updates the `category` URL parameter (e.g., navigating to faq.html?category=bundles or using history API to adjust the query string).
5) The FAQ list re-filters entries using the new category value.
6) The category filter highlights the newly selected category as active.

Follow a bulk order FAQ to the Contact page:
1) User filters by or scrolls to a FAQ entry whose `category` is related to bulk orders (e.g., "bulk_orders").
2) The answer text for that entry includes a link such as 'Contact us about bulk orders'.
3) When the user clicks this link, the browser navigates to contact.html with appropriate query parameters, for example: contact.html?topic=bulk_orders&subject=Bulk+order+for+25+paperbacks.
4) The Contact page reads these URL parameters and pre-fills its form accordingly.

Follow FAQs to Books or Events pages:
1) User opens an FAQ answer that discusses book formats, how to purchase, or upcoming events.
2) Within that answer, links such as 'Browse books' or 'View upcoming events' are rendered as standard anchors.
3) Clicking 'Browse books' navigates the user to books.html.
4) Clicking 'View upcoming events' navigates the user to events.html.
5) The user continues their task (e.g., choosing a book or registering for an event) on the respective page.

Share a direct link to a specific FAQ question:
1) User expands a particular FAQ entry in the list.
2) The entry exposes (for example) a small 'copy link' or 'link to this question' control that represents the deep link for that FAQ item.
3) When activated, the page constructs a URL including both the `category` parameter (if applicable) and an anchor using the FAQ entry’s `id` (e.g., faq.html?category=bundles#faq-{id}).
4) The user copies and shares this URL.
5) When another user visits this URL, the page loads entries with getFaqEntries, applies the `category` filter from the query string, then scrolls to and optionally auto-expands the FAQ item whose `id` matches the fragment identifier.
interactions: On page load, read the `category` URL parameter and call getFaqEntries to fetch all FAQ entries.
On successful response from getFaqEntries, compute the set of distinct `category` values for use in the category filter UI.
Clicking a category option in the category filter updates the `category` URL parameter (or adds/removes it) and triggers re-filtering of the FAQ entries.
Submitting the search form (or updating the search field) updates an in-memory search state and re-filters the currently loaded FAQ entries by question and answer text.
Clicking a question row toggles expansion/collapse of that FAQ entry’s answer without additional API calls.
Clicking a 'Contact us about bulk orders' link inside an answer navigates to contact.html with topic=bulk_orders and a predefined subject string for bulk orders.
Clicking a 'Browse books' link inside an answer navigates to books.html.
Clicking a 'View upcoming events' link inside an answer navigates to events.html.
Clicking a 'link to this question' control for a FAQ entry copies or navigates to a URL that includes the entry’s `id` as a fragment identifier and, if relevant, the `category` parameter.
Handling an invalid or unknown `category` value by showing no matching FAQs (or a fallback message) while still allowing the user to select a valid category from the category filter.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary external state for this page is managed through URL parameters and the location fragment. On initialization, the page reads `window.location.search` to obtain a `category` parameter, if present. The page then calls getFaqEntries once to retrieve all FAQ entries; that API takes no parameters. After data loading, the page maintains an in-memory list of all entries and derives a filtered list as follows: 1) If `category` is absent, all entries are eligible for display. 2) If `category` is present, only entries whose `category` field matches the parameter value exactly are eligible. 3) A separate in-memory `searchQuery` state further narrows the eligible entries by matching the query string against `question` and `answer` text fields. 4) For deep linking, the page also reads `window.location.hash` (the fragment) after filtering; if it matches a FAQ entry’s `id`, that entry is scrolled into view and may be auto-expanded. Whenever the user changes the selected category via the UI, the page updates the `category` URL parameter (e.g., rebuilding the query string and using history.pushState or a navigation to faq.html?category={value}). This ensures that the current category filter is shareable and persists across reloads. No state is persisted in localStorage or other client-side storage; the URL is the single source of truth for the category filter, while search text and expansion state remain transient in memory.

components.functionality: Coordinates data loading, URL parameter parsing, and composition of all FAQ sub-components. It initializes the page by reading the `category` query parameter, invoking getFaqEntries, and passing the loaded data and derived state (selected category, search query, filtered entries) to child components.
event_handlers: onInit: Called when faq.html loads; reads URL parameters and triggers getFaqEntries.
onGetFaqEntriesSuccess: Stores the returned entries, derives availableCategories, applies category and search filters, and triggers rendering.
onGetFaqEntriesError: Updates an error state that can be displayed to the user (e.g., a generic error message).
onChangeCategoryFromUI: Updates selectedCategory, synchronizes the `category` parameter in the URL, re-applies filters, and re-renders.
onChangeSearchQuery: Updates searchQuery, re-applies filters, and re-renders.
onHashChange: When the fragment identifier changes, checks for a matching FAQ entry `id` and requests the FAQ list to scroll and possibly auto-expand that entry.

components.functionality: Displays the set of FAQ categories derived from the loaded entries, plus an option to show all categories. Allows the user to select a category, which updates the selectedCategory state and the `category` URL parameter. It reflects the active selection visually and informs the rest of the page which category is currently active.
data_binding: availableCategories: derived from getFaqEntries() result by extracting unique `category` values
selectedCategory: derived from URLSearchParams.category or defaulted to 'all'
event_handlers: onClickCategoryOption: Receives the clicked category value, updates selectedCategory in the page container, and updates the `category` query parameter in the URL (adding, changing, or removing it as appropriate).
onSyncFromUrl: When the URL’s `category` parameter changes (e.g., via browser navigation or external link), updates the highlighted active category to remain in sync.

components.functionality: Provides a text input to filter the visible FAQ entries by keywords. The filter is applied client-side on top of the current category filter, using the loaded FAQ entries; no additional API calls are made.
data_binding: searchQuery: an in-memory string representing the current search input value
getFaqEntries() result: used indirectly to compute filteredFaqEntries based on searchQuery
event_handlers: onInputChange: Updates searchQuery as the user types and triggers re-filtering of FAQ entries (live search).
onSubmitSearch: Prevents default page reload behavior, confirms the current input as searchQuery, and re-filters FAQ entries.
onClearSearch: Resets searchQuery to an empty string and restores the view to all entries for the currently selected category.

components.functionality: Renders the list of FAQ entries that match the current category and search filters. Manages which entries are expanded or collapsed and coordinates deep-link behavior, such as auto-expanding a specific entry when its `id` matches the location fragment.
data_binding: filteredFaqEntries: subset of getFaqEntries() result after applying category and search filters
expandedEntryIds: in-memory set of entry IDs that are currently expanded
window.location.hash: used to detect if a particular entry should be auto-expanded and scrolled into view
event_handlers: onToggleEntry: Toggles an entry’s expanded/collapsed state when its question header is clicked (adds/removes the entry’s `id` from expandedEntryIds).
onReceiveDeepLinkId: When notified of a hash matching an entry `id`, ensures that entry is expanded and scrolled into view.
onClickEntryAnchorIcon: Optionally constructs and exposes a URL including the entry’s `id` as a fragment (and current `category` parameter) for sharing.

components.functionality: Represents a single FAQ entry with a clickable question and a collapsible answer. It is responsible for displaying the question text, conditionally rendering the answer when expanded, and rendering any internal links contained in the answer content.
data_binding: FaqEntry: { id, question, answer, category } from getFaqEntries() result
isExpanded: boolean derived from faq-list’s expandedEntryIds
event_handlers: onClickQuestion: Notifies the faq-list that this entry should toggle its expanded state.
onRenderAnswer: Ensures that answer content (which may include HTML/markup with links) is rendered safely and that internal links remain clickable.

onClickInternalLink: Delegates to default browser navigation for links to contact.html, books.html, and events.html, allowing URL parameters defined in those links (e.g., topic=bulk_orders&subject=Bulk+order+for+25+paperbacks) to be used by the target pages.

components.functionality: Conceptual component describing how links embedded in FAQ answers trigger navigation to other pages using URL parameters. It does not call APIs but ensures that clicks on specific links are correctly interpreted as internal navigation actions.
data_binding: anchor elements contained within the rendered answer HTML
href attributes that point to contact.html, books.html, or events.html with query parameters
event_handlers: onClickContactBulkOrdersLink: Navigates to contact.html with query parameters such as topic=bulk_orders and subject=Bulk+order+for+25+paperbacks when the user clicks the bulk-order contact link within an answer.
onClickBrowseBooksLink: Navigates to books.html when the user clicks a 'Browse books' style link in an answer.
onClickViewEventsLink: Navigates to events.html when the user clicks a 'View upcoming events' style link in an answer.

layout.grid_system: Fixed-width, center-aligned layout likely based on a 12-column grid, with a two-column main content area inside a full-width background.
layout.layout_pattern: The FAQ page keeps the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow: a full-width site header and navigation at the very top, then a large, centered black content panel sitting on the textured paper background. Inside this black panel, content is organized into a two-column layout: the left column is a single, tall navigation/utility card (replacing the portrait, but similar in shape and weight), and the right column stacks the FAQ tools and content vertically. In the right column, the FAQ category filter bar sits at the top, followed by the search form, and then the FAQ list filling the remainder. The internal navigation links live entirely in the left column, giving it a strong vertical block, while all FAQ interaction (filter, search, reading questions) happens in the right column.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The FAQ page keeps the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom flow: a full-width site header and navigation at the very top, then a large, centered black content panel sitting on the textured paper background. Inside this black panel, content is organized into a two-column layout: the left column is a single, tall navigation/utility card (replacing the portrait, but similar in shape and weight), and the right column stacks the FAQ tools and content vertically. In the right column, the FAQ category filter bar sits at the top, followed by the search form, and then the FAQ list filling the remainder. The internal navigation links live entirely in the left column, giving it a strong vertical block, while all FAQ interaction (filter, search, reading questions) happens in the right column.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header occupies roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport height. Below it, the main black FAQ panel dominates the remaining vertical space, inset from the viewport edges by about 32–48px on all sides, so the crumpled paper texture frames it. Inside the black panel, there is 24–32px of internal padding. The two inner columns mirror the mockup: the left column takes about 40–45% of the panel width, the right column about 55–60%, with a 24px gutter between them. At the top of the panel, a section heading (part of the page container) spans both columns. Directly under this, the left column is almost entirely occupied by the internal navigation links card, while in the right column, the upper 20–25% of that column’s height is shared by the category filter bar and search form, and the remaining 75–80% is occupied by the FAQ list. On smaller screens, these columns stack into a single column that spans roughly 90–100% of the panel width.
layout_strategies.content_density: The header remains relatively spacious, with generous padding around the author name, tagline, and navigation. Inside the black panel, the content becomes more compact to achieve a medium-to-high density: small vertical gaps (8–16px) between the filter bar, search form, and FAQ items; moderate internal padding of 16–24px inside each card; and tight stacking of FAQ items to allow many questions to be visible at once. The left navigation column has a bit more breathing room between links to keep it legible as a vertical anchor, while the right column is intentionally denser to emphasize functionality and information.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual entry still starts at the bright, centered author name and tagline in the full-width header, then moves horizontally along the navigation where the 'FAQ' item is highlighted similarly to how 'CONTACT' is in the mockup. From there, the eye drops down to the large, framed black panel and lands first on the central 'Frequently Asked Questions' heading. The gaze then moves left to the tall internal navigation links card (the equivalent of the portrait column), then sweeps horizontally to the right toward the bold category filter bar. The next pull is downward to the search form just beneath the filter, and finally the eye tracks down the left edges of the stacked FAQ items within the list, reading each question and its answer. This creates a similar F-shaped pattern: top header and nav, then across the top of the FAQ panel via the heading and filter, and then down the left-aligned questions in the FAQ list.

layout.overall_description: Below the dramatic, full-width header and navigation bar, the FAQ page presents a large, centered black panel floating on the textured paper background, with 32–48px of paper margin on all sides. The panel itself is fixed-width and center-aligned, with about 24–32px of internal padding. At the very top of this panel, a bold, literary-style 'Frequently Asked Questions' heading spans across, setting the tone. Beneath this heading, the panel splits into two columns: on the left, a single tall card holding internal navigation links to key sections like Books, Events, and Contact, as well as in-page anchors for different FAQ sections; on the right, a functional stack made up of a horizontal category filter bar, a compact search field, and a dense list of FAQ items. The left column occupies roughly 40–45% of the panel’s width and extends downward with a visually solid block, while the right column uses about 55–60% of the width, filled with interactive controls and scrollable content. The FAQ category filter stretches across the top of the right column as a strong horizontal strip, the search field sits just below it, and the FAQ list flows downwards, with each FAQ item forming a compact black-on-dark card with clear typographic hierarchy and subtle borders. The combination of the framed black panel, two-column structure, and stacked interactive elements recreates the moody, high-contrast, F-shaped reading pattern of the original contact mockup but tuned to browsing and filtering questions instead of sending a message.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ page container is the central black panel that lives directly beneath the site-wide header and navigation. It is fixed-width and center-aligned on the page, occupying roughly 70–80% of the viewport width on desktop (around 960–1120px), with equal left and right margins so that the textured paper background forms a visible frame around it. There is about 32–48px of vertical space between the bottom edge of the header and the top edge of this panel. Inside the panel, content is inset by 24–32px of padding on all sides. At the top of the container, a bold 'Frequently Asked Questions' heading is centered horizontally and spans across where both columns will be, with about 16–24px of space below it. Directly beneath that heading, the interior splits into two columns: a left column taking about 40–45% of the usable width and a right column taking about 55–60%, separated by a 24px gutter. The left column is reserved for the internal navigation links card, which runs from just under the heading down to near the bottom padding, creating a tall, continuous block. The right column stacks the FAQ category filter at the top, with the search form directly below it, followed by the FAQ list extending toward the bottom padding. On tablet and mobile, the container keeps its top and bottom margins but the two columns collapse into a single column: first the heading, then the internal navigation links full-width, then the category filter, search, and FAQ list full-width beneath it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a standard, scrollable content container in the normal document flow; its height grows with content. It remains centered on the page at all times and does not use sticky or fixed positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ category filter is positioned at the top of the right column inside the black panel, aligned with the top edge of the internal navigation links card on the left. It sits approximately 16–24px below the main 'Frequently Asked Questions' heading. The filter spans the full width of the right column (about 55–60% of the panel’s inner width) and appears as a horizontal bar of category options (e.g., Books, Formats, Bundles, Events, Ordering). The bar has a height of roughly 48–64px, with 12–16px of padding above and below the category labels, and 16–24px of horizontal padding within the bar. Individual category pills or tabs are arranged in a left-to-right row, with 8–12px of space between them, and the currently active category is highlighted in a red or orange accent similar to the mockup’s CTA buttons. There is a small vertical gap of about 8–12px between the bottom of this filter bar and the top of the search form below, keeping the right column compact but legible. On mobile, the filter bar becomes full-width and may wrap its category pills into two lines while maintaining the same compact vertical rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scrolls with the rest of the right column content in the normal flow. It remains at the top of the right column section but is not fixed or sticky by default.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ search form sits directly beneath the category filter in the right column, maintaining the stacked layout. It is separated from the filter bar by a tight 8–12px vertical gap to reflect the mockup’s medium-to-high content density. The search form uses roughly 70–100% of the right column’s width; on larger screens it can be aligned slightly to the right with 16–24px of breathing room on the left, while on narrower screens it expands to full column width for ease of use. The form itself is a single, horizontally oriented search field, about 40–48px tall, with an icon on the left and a small red or orange search button on the right. The field has around 8px of internal horizontal padding for the text and is framed by a subtle light border to stand out from the black panel. There is about 16–24px of space between the bottom of the search form and the first item in the FAQ list below, keeping the top of the right column visually organized: bar (filter), then control (search), then content (list). On mobile, the search form sits directly under the category filter filling the available width, with the same compact vertical spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of the standard flow within the right column; it does not float or stick. It moves up and down as the user scrolls the FAQ content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ list occupies all remaining vertical space in the right column beneath the search form, becoming the largest continuous content block on that side. It begins about 16–24px below the search form, matching the internal padding pattern of the panel. The list spans the full width of the right column and stacks FAQ items vertically from top to bottom, forming a column of compact question cards. Each item is flush-left with the right column’s content edge, and the list extends down to within 24–32px of the bottom edge of the black panel, respecting the container’s internal padding. The FAQ list is designed to feel dense but orderly: items are separated by 8–12px of vertical space, so multiple questions are visible within the viewport at once, reinforcing the information-rich character of the page. On smaller screens, the FAQ list becomes a full-width stack beneath the filters and search, maintaining the same spacing but using the full width of the panel so that question text remains readable.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The list grows vertically with the number of FAQ items and scrolls naturally with the page. It does not use internal scrolling; instead, the entire page scroll controls access to all FAQs.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each FAQ item is a compact card within the FAQ list that spans nearly the full width of the right column (minus the list’s internal padding). Visually, it appears as a dark card or bordered row set against the black panel, with a subtle lighter border or bottom rule and 12–16px of internal padding. At the top of the item, the question is presented in a slightly larger, bold serif or strong sans-serif type, aligned to the left, with a small chevron or plus icon aligned to the far right indicating expand/collapse state. This top line forms a clean horizontal band roughly 40–48px in height. When collapsed, only this question row is visible. When expanded, the answer text appears directly below the question within the same card, indented slightly from the question’s left edge by about 8–16px and using a smaller, regular-weight font. The answer block has 8–12px of top padding separating it from the question line and 8–12px of bottom padding before the card ends. Vertically, each FAQ item is separated from the next by 8–12px, keeping the stack tight while still visually distinct. The overall effect is a tall column of frequently asked questions that encourages scanning down the left edge of questions in an F-shaped pattern, with answers revealed on demand.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Each item expands and collapses vertically within the list when interacted with, pushing adjacent items up or down; there is no overlay or floating behavior. It remains within the normal document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The internal navigation links component occupies the entire left column inside the black panel, forming a tall, portrait-like card that mirrors the single, vertical element in the mockup’s left side. It begins just below the main 'Frequently Asked Questions' heading, with about 16–24px of space between the heading and the top of this card. The card uses the full width of the left column (about 40–45% of the panel width) and extends downward, ideally reaching close to the bottom padding of the panel so it reads as a continuous vertical block. Inside the card, there is 16–24px of padding on all sides. The content is a vertical list of links grouped into sections: for example, primary links to other site pages (Books, Events, Contact) at the top, followed by in-page anchor links to major FAQ categories (Books & Series, Formats & Devices, Bundles & Offers, Events & Signings, Ordering & Shipping). Each link line is left-aligned, with 8–12px of vertical space between lines, and active or current contexts (such as a link to the FAQ section currently in view) can be accented in red to echo the highlighted navigation item in the header. The typography is bold and legible, making this column a strong visual anchor. On mobile, this navigation card appears directly beneath the FAQ heading and above the filter and search controls, spanning the full width of the panel, but it retains approximately the same internal padding and vertical rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The navigation links card scrolls with the rest of the page in the normal flow. It remains fixed in the left column on desktop layouts but does not stick to the viewport when scrolling by default.

name: Policies
title: Policies
description: Displays the site's privacy, data usage, terms of use, and review/comment guidelines, with deep-link navigation via URL parameters and a contact link for policy questions.

core_features: Fetch and render all policy content (privacy policy, data usage summary, terms of use, and review/comment guidelines) using the getPoliciesContent interface.
Provide section-based navigation so users can jump directly to specific policy sections (e.g., privacy, terms, reviews).
Support deep-linking by reading the optional URL parameter ?section=... and automatically scrolling to the corresponding policy section.
Offer an explicit "Questions about these policies" interaction that navigates to contact.html with topic=other.
Allow in-page navigation between sections without reloading, while updating the URL parameter to keep links shareable.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: General access from footer link
1. User clicks the "Policies" link in the footer (no section parameter).
2. Browser navigates to policies.html.
3. policies-page-controller component initializes, parses the URL (no section found) and calls getPoliciesContent.
4. When getPoliciesContent resolves, the controller stores the policy content in page state and renders all policy sections in sequence.
5. Since no section parameter is provided, the page scroll position remains at the top and no specific section is highlighted.
6. User uses the in-page navigation (policies-toc) to jump between sections; each click updates ?section=... in the URL and scrolls to the selected section.

Workflow 2: Arrive from a book review form (reviews section)
1. On the book_detail.html review form, the user clicks a link such as "Read review guidelines" that navigates to policies.html?section=reviews.
2. policies-page-controller initializes, parses section=reviews and calls getPoliciesContent.
3. After getPoliciesContent returns, the controller renders all sections and then scrolls to the reviews section anchor.
4. The corresponding TOC entry and section (reviews) are marked as active in the UI state.
5. User reads the review guidelines and may navigate to other sections via the TOC; each TOC click updates the URL parameter and scrolls accordingly.
6. When done, the user returns to the previous page (e.g., using the browser Back button) and continues submitting their review.

Workflow 3: Arrive from a blog comment form (privacy or terms section)
1. On article_detail.html, near the comment form, the user clicks a link to policies.html with a specific section (e.g., policies.html?section=privacy or policies.html?section=terms).
2. policies-page-controller parses the URL, stores the requested section, and calls getPoliciesContent.
3. After content is loaded and rendered, the controller scrolls to the requested section.
4. The user reviews the relevant privacy or terms information, and may optionally navigate to the reviews section to check comment rules.
5. User returns to article_detail.html (browser Back) to continue writing or submitting their comment.

Workflow 4: Ask a question about policies
1. User is on policies.html and has a question or finds content unclear.
2. User clicks a "Questions about these policies?" or similar link rendered by policies-contact-link.
3. The click handler navigates to contact.html?topic=other.
4. contact.html reads topic=other and preselects or prepopulates the topic as "other" in its contact form, so the user can submit a policy-related question.
interactions: On initial page load, policies-page-controller reads the "section" URL parameter (if present), stores it as requestedSection, and then calls getPoliciesContent once to fetch all policy texts.
If getPoliciesContent succeeds, the controller passes the resulting privacyPolicyText, dataUsageSummary, termsOfUseText, and reviewGuidelines to the policies-section-block components for rendering.

If getPoliciesContent fails or returns an unexpected structure, the controller sets an error state that can be rendered as a generic error message and prevents section navigation until a retry mechanism (e.g., manual reload) is used.

When the user clicks an entry in the policies-toc component, an onSectionSelect event is fired with the selected section key (e.g., "privacy", "terms", "reviews"). The controller then updates the URL to policies.html?section=<selected> via the History API (or full navigation) and scrolls to the corresponding section anchor.

When the URL changes (either via navigation from another page or via in-page interactions updating the "section" parameter), policies-page-controller reconciles activeSection with the new parameter and triggers scroll-to-anchor behavior without refetching content if it is already loaded.
When the user clicks the policies-contact-link component, an onClick handler triggers navigation to contact.html?topic=other without altering the current policies.html URL state.

When a user manually edits the URL (e.g., changes ?section=privacy to ?section=unknown) and reloads, the controller detects that the provided value does not match any known section and falls back to showing content from the top without attempting a targeted scroll.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page uses the query parameter "section" in the policies.html URL to control which section is considered active and to manage initial scroll behavior. On page initialization, policies-page-controller parses window.location.search to extract section. If the value matches a known section key (e.g., "privacy", "terms", "reviews" or any other internally defined section IDs corresponding to the API return fields), it is stored as requestedSection. The controller then calls getPoliciesContent exactly once to retrieve policy texts. When content is loaded, the controller computes activeSection: if requestedSection is valid, activeSection = requestedSection; otherwise, activeSection is null and the page remains at the top. The controller triggers a scroll to the DOM element whose id or data attribute matches activeSection. The policies-toc component also uses activeSection to visually indicate which section is active. When the user clicks a TOC item, the click handler calls back into the controller with a newSection value. The controller updates activeSection to newSection, updates the URL query parameter (either via history.pushState or a full navigation) to reflect ?section=newSection, and scrolls to the corresponding section. The policy content itself is not stored in URL parameters; it remains in in-memory state populated from getPoliciesContent. No localStorage or sessionStorage is used; all state is derived from the current URL and in-memory variables for the current page session.

components.functionality: Coordinates data fetching from getPoliciesContent, parses and reacts to the "section" URL parameter, manages active section state, and passes policy text to child components.
Renders an in-page navigation list of available policy sections and allows users to jump to a specific section. Highlights the currently active section based on state from the controller.

Displays the privacy policy section content using the privacyPolicyText returned from getPoliciesContent. Exposes an anchor with a stable identifier (e.g., id="privacy") for deep linking and scrolling.

Displays data usage information (how reviews, reading lists, registration details, etc. are handled) using the dataUsageSummary value from getPoliciesContent. Provides an anchor to support optional deep linking if mapped to a section key.

Displays the terms of use content (site usage terms, disclaimers, legal notices related to purchases, events, or communications) using the termsOfUseText from getPoliciesContent. Exposes an anchor with a stable identifier (e.g., id="terms").

Displays review and comment guidelines related to submitting book reviews and blog comments using the reviewGuidelines from getPoliciesContent. Exposes an anchor with a stable identifier (e.g., id="reviews") to support links from review and comment forms.
Provides a clear action for users who have questions about the policies, navigating them to the contact page with the topic preset to "other".

Displays a generic error or loading status when getPoliciesContent is in progress or fails so that users understand the page state (e.g., loading, retry needed). Does not contain any hardcoded policy content.
Handles scrolling to section anchors based on activeSection and listens for intersection/visibility changes from policy-section-* components to update activeSection when the user scrolls manually.

layout.grid_system: Fixed-width, center-aligned layout likely based on a 12-column grid, with a two-column main content area inside a full-width background.
layout.layout_pattern: Replicate the mockup’s structure: a full-width site header and nav at the very top (outside this component set), followed by a large, centered black panel sitting on the crumpled paper background. Inside this panel, use a two-column layout: the left column becomes a tall, vertically stacked policies table of contents (section navigation), and the right column holds the main policy content sections (Privacy, Data Usage, Terms, Reviews) stacked top-to-bottom. The page controller, error state, contact link, and scroll behavior all live within or above this panel in a way that preserves the linear top-to-bottom reading order.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Replicate the mockup’s structure: a full-width site header and nav at the very top (outside this component set), followed by a large, centered black panel sitting on the crumpled paper background. Inside this panel, use a two-column layout: the left column becomes a tall, vertically stacked policies table of contents (section navigation), and the right column holds the main policy content sections (Privacy, Data Usage, Terms, Reviews) stacked top-to-bottom. The page controller, error state, contact link, and scroll behavior all live within or above this panel in a way that preserves the linear top-to-bottom reading order.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The fixed-width black content panel is centered and occupies roughly 70–80% of the viewport width, with a 32–48px margin of textured background on all sides to create a framed effect. Inside the panel, the left TOC column takes about 35–40% of the internal width, and the right policy content column takes 60–65%. Vertically, the top 10–15% of the panel is reserved for the main page title and any error/status messaging above the sections; the rest is used by the four policy content sections, which extend below the fold. The contact link is anchored near the bottom-right of the panel, following the same visual line as the mockup’s primary call-to-action button.
layout_strategies.content_density: Within the black panel, maintain a medium-to-high content density similar to the mockup’s contact form: compact vertical spacing and tight typographic rhythm. Section headings have clear separation (24–32px above, 16–24px below), but paragraphs and lists within each section use relatively small gaps (8–16px) to keep the policies feeling like a continuous, serious document. The TOC column is dense but scannable, with links stacked closely (8–12px apart). The outer frame (background around the panel) remains more spacious to contrast with the content-heavy interior.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Preserve the F-shaped visual flow from the mockup. After the user’s eye is drawn to the bright site title and red-highlighted 'POLICIES' navigation item in the global header, it drops to the centered black panel. Inside the panel, the eye first catches the page heading in the top-left of the right column, then moves left to the TOC list, scanning down the stacked entries. From there, attention sweeps horizontally to the right onto the first policy section heading and then downward through the body text, following each section in sequence. The contact link at the bottom-right echoes the mockup’s 'Send' button position, serving as the final focal point for users needing clarification. The scroll manager and sticky TOC reinforce this left-then-right, top-to-bottom reading pattern as the user navigates between sections.

layout.overall_description: The Policies page sits within the same dark, dramatic frame as the rest of the author site: a full-width header and navigation bar at the top over a textured, crumpled paper background, with the current 'POLICIES' nav item highlighted in red. Below, a large, matte-black content panel is centered on the page, leaving a generous border of textured paper around it. Inside this panel, the content is divided into two distinct columns. On the left, a tall, narrow column presents a vertical list of policy sections as a table of contents, with strong typographic styling and subtle dividers that feel like chapter markers in a book. On the right, a wider column holds the core of the page: a 'Policies & Terms' heading near the top, followed by individually titled sections for Privacy, Data Usage, Terms of Use, and Reviews/Comments, each separated by modest spacing and thin rules to maintain a dense, document-like flow. Any loading or routing errors appear as a red-accented alert bar directly under the page heading, spanning the content column. As the user scrolls, the left TOC remains visible, pinning itself below the header so that section links stay accessible while the right column scrolls through the text. At the end of the last section, in the bottom-right corner of the panel, a subtle yet clearly styled 'Contact us about these policies' link echoes the placement of the contact page’s 'Send' button, guiding users toward follow-up contact without breaking the serious, literary mood. The entire layout feels like reading a carefully typeset page from a dark, modern legal codex, framed by the author’s atmospheric brand.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page controller is an invisible layer that governs the layout and behavior of the central black panel and its contents. Conceptually, it sits just inside the top edge of the black content panel, spanning the full width of that panel (roughly 70–80% of the viewport, center-aligned) and extending vertically to cover the entire area taken by the TOC and policy sections. It determines which policy section is initially in view based on URL parameters (e.g., ?section=privacy) and ensures the right column scrolls so that the matching section heading appears near the top of the visible content area, just below the global header and any fixed TOC. When an error state needs to be shown (such as an invalid section ID), the controller reserves a horizontal strip beneath the main page heading in the right column for the error bar, pushing the first policy section slightly downward while keeping the left TOC aligned vertically. The controller also manages responsive adjustments: below a narrow width threshold, it can change the two-column layout into a stacked layout (TOC on top, sections below) while preserving the same top-to-bottom flow and maintaining the textured paper frame around the black panel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, structural controller that influences scroll positioning, initial section focus, and how the main panel responds to viewport changes; it affects but does not occupy visible space on its own.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policies table of contents occupies the entire left column inside the black panel. It begins approximately 24px below the inner top edge of the panel, aligning visually with the baseline of the main 'Policies & Terms' heading in the right column. Horizontally, it starts about 24px in from the left edge of the black panel, leaving a consistent inner padding. The TOC column takes up about 35–40% of the panel’s internal width, with a 24–32px gap separating it from the right content column. Within this column, section links are stacked vertically like a chapter list: each item occupies a single line or two, with 8–12px vertical spacing between items and occasional subtle separators (such as a faint horizontal line or small bullet) every 2–3 items to echo the mockup’s use of borders. The top of the TOC may include a small label such as 'On this page' in a muted white or grey, with individual section links in white text and a red highlight or underline on the active section, reinforcing the red navigation cues in the header. On taller pages, as the user scrolls down through the policy text, the TOC itself becomes sticky: after the global header scrolls off, the TOC column pins itself so that its top edge sits just below the header area (approximately 16–24px offset), remaining fixed in place while the right column scrolls. This creates a tall, continuous left strip of navigation mirroring the static, tall portrait on the contact page, anchoring the layout visually.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as a sticky sidebar within the black panel: initially scrolls with the page, then becomes fixed within the panel once it reaches a predefined offset beneath the global header; width remains constant relative to the panel while height adapts to its content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Privacy section is the first and uppermost content block in the right column of the black panel. It starts approximately 24px below the top inner padding of the panel, horizontally aligned with the TOC’s first navigation item. The right column begins about 24–32px to the right of the TOC column, occupying roughly 60–65% of the panel’s internal width. At its top, a strong section heading (e.g., 'Privacy Policy') is set in bold, high-contrast type, spanning nearly the full width of the right column. Above this heading, a slightly larger page-level title like 'Policies & Terms' may sit, with 8–12px spacing separating it from the section heading, giving this first section a visually prominent entry point. Below the 'Privacy Policy' heading, the body content (paragraphs, bullet lists, subheadings) flows downward with 8–16px spacing between text blocks, emphasizing a dense, document-like reading experience. The total vertical space for this section extends down until a 32px gap before the next section heading. A thin, subtle horizontal divider line in muted grey or deep red may be placed at the bottom of the section to clearly separate Privacy from Data Usage without breaking the dark, literary aesthetic. On narrow viewports where the layout stacks, Privacy still appears directly under the TOC, preserving the expectation that it is the first detailed section the user encounters.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level content that scrolls with the page; its top edge is a target for deep-link and smooth-scroll behavior, aligning near the top of the visible area when navigated to from the TOC or URL.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Data Usage section is positioned directly beneath the Privacy section within the right column, maintaining the same horizontal alignment and width. It begins after a consistent vertical gap of around 32px from the end of the Privacy content (or beneath a subtle horizontal divider line if used). The section heading (e.g., 'Data Usage & Cookies') mirrors the typographic treatment of the Privacy heading, using bold, high-contrast text that stretches nearly the full column width. Content within this section is organized into short paragraphs and, where appropriate, structured lists or subheadings describing what data is collected, how it is used, and retention policies. Internal spacing remains tight—8–16px between pieces of content—to maintain the compact, information-rich feel. Like the section above, the bottom of Data Usage is marked by a 32px vertical space before the next section begins, optionally punctuated by a faint divider line. In the overall column height, Data Usage occupies the second major block of content, meaning that when users select it from the TOC, the scroll manager ensures its heading rises to the top of the visible content window, just under the sticky header and TOC alignment, continuing the consistent visual rhythm down the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static content block that participates in smooth-scroll behavior and becomes the vertical focal point when targeted from the TOC; otherwise scrolls naturally as part of the right column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Terms section sits below the Data Usage section in the right column, maintaining the same left and right margins and full column width. It begins approximately 32px beneath the end of the Data Usage text (or below its divider). The section heading (e.g., 'Terms of Use') is slightly more emphatic in tone, reflecting its legal weight—possibly set in the same size as other section titles but paired with a slightly stronger color accent or accompanying thin red underline to echo the importance of the 'CONTACT' link in the main navigation. The body of this section is typically longer than the previous ones, and may be broken into clearly labeled subsections (e.g., 'Use of Content', 'User Responsibilities'), each separated by 16–24px vertical gaps, balancing dense text with enough whitespace to avoid overwhelming the reader. This section may extend significantly below the initial viewport height, but it remains bounded at its bottom by a 32px gap before the Reviews section. The entire Terms block visually reads as the central, weighty part of the page’s scroll, making the right column feel like the main text of an old legal manuscript laid out against a dark background.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static content block that may occupy more vertical space than other sections; it remains part of the normal scroll flow and is an anchor target for TOC and URL-based navigation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Reviews/Comments policy section is placed at the bottom of the right column, directly underneath the Terms section. It starts after a consistent 32px vertical gap (with or without a divider) and uses a heading such as 'Reviews & Comments Policy' styled identically to the other section headings. The content here may be more concise, outlining moderation rules, acceptable behavior, and how reviews may be displayed. This section is slightly more compact, often occupying less vertical space than the Terms section, which creates a gentle visual tapering toward the bottom of the panel. Within this section, the final paragraph or a short concluding line can visually lead the eye toward the policies-contact-link, which is positioned immediately following this section’s content, right-aligned. The bottom edge of the Reviews section maintains 16–24px spacing above the contact link, ensuring both are visually connected yet distinct. Overall, this section functions as the final major content block in the right column, just before the call-to-contact action.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static content block that defines the upper boundary of the bottom call-to-contact area; scrolls normally and serves as the last anchor target in the TOC.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policies contact link is positioned at the lower-right corner of the black panel’s interior, directly beneath the Reviews/Comments section. After the last line of the Reviews section, there is a modest vertical gap of 16–24px, then the contact link is aligned flush to the right margin of the right column, approximately 24–32px in from the panel’s right edge. Horizontally, it does not span the full width; instead, it reads as a compact text-based call to action (e.g., 'Contact us about these policies') styled like a subtle button or prominent link: white or light grey text with a red accent in the underline, arrow, or hover state, echoing the orange/red button prominence from the contact page but toned down to suit a more informational context. The left side of this link aligns roughly with the center of the right column, leaving empty space between the left TOC and the link to emphasize its position as an endpoint in the visual flow. There is 24–32px of padding between the link and the bottom edge of the black panel, preventing it from feeling cramped. On smaller screens, when the layout stacks, this link appears after all sections, still right-aligned relative to the main content width, maintaining its role as the final interactive element on the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static link that scrolls into view at the end of the content; functions as the final focal point in the vertical flow, with no sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error state appears as a horizontal alert bar in the upper portion of the right column, just below the page-level title ('Policies & Terms') and above the Privacy section heading. It spans the full width of the right column (approximately 60–65% of the panel’s width) and sits flush with the left and right inner edges of that column. Vertically, the alert bar is relatively compact—about 40–64px in height—containing a brief error message such as 'We couldn’t find that policy section. Showing all policies instead.' Its background uses a dark red or deep charcoal tone with a thin brighter red border, aligning with the site’s red accent language while maintaining legibility against the black panel. There is 16px of vertical spacing above and below the bar so it doesn’t crowd the headings or the start of the Privacy content. When visible, it pushes the content sections downward, preserving the overall alignment between the right column and the left TOC. When no error is present, this bar collapses entirely, allowing the Privacy section to move up beneath the page title, and the vertical rhythm remains clean. On very narrow viewports, the error bar remains placed just beneath the page title and above all sections, spanning the full available width.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and disappears dynamically; when active, it occupies fixed vertical space, pushing the main content down. It scrolls with the rest of the page and does not remain fixed or sticky.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scroll manager is a non-visual behavioral layer that coordinates how the left TOC and right content column interact during navigation. Spatially, it can be thought of as covering the entire black panel and monitoring the positions of each policy section relative to the viewport. When a user clicks a TOC item, the scroll manager triggers a smooth vertical scroll that moves the page so that the corresponding section heading (Privacy, Data Usage, Terms, Reviews) aligns near the top of the right column’s visible area, just below the global header and any sticky TOC offset. It also updates the TOC highlighting to reflect the currently prominent section, ensuring that as users manually scroll through the right column, the active TOC item changes when a section’s heading crosses a certain threshold (e.g., one-third from the top of the viewport). Additionally, the scroll manager can adjust for the presence of the error bar and sticky TOC, slightly tweaking where sections land so they are not hidden behind fixed elements. On long documents, this creates a fluid, document-like navigation experience where the left navigation rail and the right content remain synchronized, reinforcing the F-shaped pattern down and across the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Invisible behavior controller that governs smooth scrolling, active section detection, and TOC highlighting; it does not occupy visible space but affects the spatial relationship between sections and the viewport during interaction.
